name: Big Ideas and Themes.md
text:
## Big Ideas and Themes

1. Events from the past make us who we are today
   1. Today is real, the past is not
   2. Every event leading to right now was determined by some prior series of events
   3. The state of the world at any given instant is entirely predetermined 
      1. If everything is predetermined, can you blame someone for actions they are taking right now?
2. Subconsciousness predates language
3. Everyone wants to carry on even after they die. To have an impact. To leave a legacy.
   1. Not everyone has the same level of concern with their legacy.
      1. Some want to maximize their daily happiness at the expense of their legacy/impact
   2. For most, having children is their legacy. Its how they can impact this world and its future turns. 
   3. Others think bigger. Want to impact the the world in ways other than immediate friends and family
      1. Starting a company 
      2. Writing a book
      3. Inventing something
      4. Solving the worlds big problems - in some scale or another
4. Does modern existentialism spring from our digitized world? Or have we simply reached the evolution of the human condition? 
   1. Were we always destined to reach this point? Are humans fundamentally ill-equipped to making decisions in a world with so much choice and freedom? 
   2. How have relative forms of happiness evolved in the modern era?

name: Brainstorming Plots.md
text:
## Brainstorming Plots

1. Subliminal search for beauty

   - something set out west, either in the style of *The Crossing* or *Less Than Zero*

2. Mesh of concept from end of '*This Side of Paradise*' and Nietzsche

   - 'Ought' —> 'Owe' (source of morals)
   - The 'young' are willing to object to predetermined ideals, they are the only ones who seem to have the ability
   - 'Good' vs 'Evil' — to question and not merely accept
   - an objection to the current thought that 'find your own way' and 'you don't have to be a doctor'
     - show that the effect of being a constant 'debtor' to the 'creditor' that is our parents and today's expectations has pushed us to this mindset
     - should be raised with an aptitude to question and contemplate rather than resign and accept
   - by pushing our generation to this depth, no one knows what they really want and this leads to a generation of weak willed souls — unwilling to strive for success because they haven't been allowed to determine what success is for themselves
   - generation after generation vehemently attest to those younger than themselves because they are inherently regretful (jaded)
   - pursuing success for the sake of success
     - meanwhile, everyone else asks what the point of it all is

3. Something told from the perspective of a Roman emperor as his city falls

4. Main character witnesses a girl he is secretly falling for get raped

   - Over time, the memory fades, but when she begins to show an interest years later, those feelings of attraction aren't the same
   - Maybe they date for a while, but eventually the time comes to get engaged or move on
   - A dominant masculine protective instinct is prevalent
   - He doesn't want a 'wife', but rather a 'partner'

5. Dream State

   - High Level
     - Taking more drastic actions to return to sleep and the dreams
     - Ominous and mysterious fact lies at the deepest level of the dreams
   - Theme
     - If we are just neurons firing in concise intervals, than is life deterministic? Are we the makers of our own fate? Is free will a lie we tell ourselves because the alternative is too hard of a truth to bear?  
     -  When we see the pointlessness of the human experience we sink into a existential depression
       - Thereby is the fact that we believe we have free will an emotional response to the cynicism that comes along with existentialism? Aka, if you get depressed when thinking about a deterministic life than are we believers in free will because it makes us non-sad? 
       - Is this just ignorance? Believe/know one thing and act another?
     - Yet there are commonalities among all humans (regardless of biology)
       - We all like being happy. We all _want_ to be happy. We all want to laugh. All want to love. 
       - Is this just chemicals in the brain? Or is the common human experience one granted by that of a maker? Of a God unknown? 
     - Is the resolution of the man to not know the answers to his questions but to accept the existence of something else above us? Or does he neglect this entirely and resolve to a life of shared _good_ experience and maximizing thereof? 
   - Structure
     - Setup
       - Need to set the scene of someone going through the motions. His stress and insomnia stems from something that even he is not sure of. 
       - At some point resorts to substances and has his first dream/vision
         - TODO: dream idea here
     - Buildup
       - Days meld together as his insomnia worsens
       - Some nights he doesnt dream at all -- and others he dreams vivid
         - TODO: need to carve out what these dreams are trying to say (and say it subtly)
     - Climax
       - TODO
     - Closing Out
       - Needs to be some sort of realization/actualization of the life worth living
       - Close on (another) dream?
     
   - Some plot:

     - He had watched Jerry Springer the night before. Who regularly watches that shit? I guess the "wow" factor probably plays an aspect to it, but you'd think folks would get tired of the false plotlines and even less adept caricatures. Oh well. For every obscure interest there be a fandom. 

       The man had just arrived at work. Entering the building always settled his mind down to the basics. Punch in. Grab a vest. Get to work. It was almost like therapy honestly. He found his station. The cab doors his company manufactured were sold to equipment companies like John Deere and Case IH. His role was to inspect the cab doors when they finished assembly and certify them as either PASS or FAIL depending on whether the product was safe to use. It was easy work, low stress, and he also felt obligated to do good work given the ramifications of shipping a defunct product.  Just yesterday he'd failed a handful of cab doors because upstream assembly had failed to secure the lugs on the handle. 

6. You're at a wedding. Something happens to/with a girl. The next morning you find her bra hanging/laying somewhere. 
   1. Something to do with the implied sexual nature of that scene

7. Man quits his big tech job and moves to Montana in search of meaning. 
          - Brings Nietzsche, Joyce, Keats, etc
              - Examines the beliefs 
              - Gets lost in the natural beauty 
              - Simplicity is what quells the anxiety 
              - Profundity in the gurgle of the creek
              - Interspersed with existential dreams 

8. Shrinking your world to the point where you become a 'God'
   1. Being very high aptitude/smart and living in a small town
   2. Autistic and becoming a domineering force amongst bugs, animals, etc
   3. Getting married to a timid girl and having 15 children whom you order around and indoctrinate
   4. Marry a timid woman and move out to no where. Spend all your time beating, sleeping, and forcing into submission

9. Being a coke addict

   1. High Level
         1. 'Drugged' lifestyle and mindset aspect to the storytelling
         2. Doing nothing and loving it every day
   2. Plot Devices
         1. Doing coke at the pizza joint
         2. Getting "massages"
         3. Monitoring your gf
               1. Dark web + passwords
               2. Cloning her phone
         4. Staying in touch with your ex (who has a new bf)
         5. Stealing drugs from your grandma
         6. Doing coke, going home with the one who's always down, getting jerked off on the couch while her roomate sits on her phone across the living room
         7. Staying awake for days on end, trying to find the "shadow people"
   1. Plot
         1. Chapter One
               1. Out at Ziggys with the gang on a weekday
               2. Go home, can't sleep. Monitoring girlfriend's phone and location. Hoovering lines and sipping whiskey. 
               3. Schedule an early morning massage. Arrive, wait for cue, roll over and get jerked off. 
               4. Go home, coming down, pass out. 

10. Drinking beer in a garage and fixing an old car with your buddy

11. Divorced parents, family dynamics

12. Pushing someone beyond their boundaries as a power play to seduce them

13. Going through a kids ride on LSD and imagining all sorts of fucked up stuff 

14. Quitting job and proceeding to do crazier and crazier shit. Escalates

name: Thoughts.md
text:
## Thoughts

- Maybe religions exist to check our pleasures. Overwrought indulgence bringing about slovenly lifestyle. Unbecoming for the children of God.
- The bones of the prophet. Buried in the Earth with those that came before. No water. No gust of wind resurface them. Their words already spoken. 
- Could these steps lead to such a high world? 
- Do the rich reach existentialism before the proletariat? 
- Bald headed birds spiral the canyon. With folded wings they fall. Below they flee. For life, for love, say thee. But a few stand with arms outstretched. To be chosen by the Gods. 
- Do meteors pass through the Heavens on their Earthward descent? Is that why they gleam with compounds not of this world?
- Taste the forbidden fruit and forever have a proclivity for the ordained? Or raze the orchard to the ground? First question of conscience. 
- Is America only considered “the land of opportunity” due to its infancy? Are we to discover water on the moon that begets “a new world”?
- Mans reach … EXCEEDS HIS IMAGINATION
- What if the old women cutting hair are sculpting us in styles they find attractive? Are we mere caricatures for a cougar’s desire? 
- If time is relative to the speed of light -- it's no wonder summer flies by. 
- Are the great directors of today the genius’s of literature in a different era?
- That nostalgic feeling when you’re younger siblings go back to college after a summer of $8 an hour and infinite opportunity. 
- What are words, but the manifestation of feelings? Maybe those best spoken are ample interpreters of they themselves.
- Imagine what this new world must have looked like to it’s frontiersman. To the earliest brigade. 
- Those from small towns descend from someone who decided to live no where. 
- What be the state of the world when your family crossed the Atlantic?
- The days of unmarked gravel roads.
- The old dreams were good dreams. And I'm glad I had them. 
- To ancient evenings and distant music.
- What resides outside your corner office? 
- Is the universe expanding at the speed of light? Or is the expansion of the universe driving our perception of this continuum?
- Adam and Eve were the last to be created by the hands of God. 
- When we pass the Turing Test we become Gods — having created Men
- If you leave two AI alone, do they speak English? Or their own language?
- Are AI’s without emotions just capitalistic machines? 
- When animals go extinct, have they simply reached their evolutionary fate? Is that where we’re headed with AI? 
- What if we as humans are the evolution of some AI-equivalent? Were we manufactured in a bottle? Have our core frequencies been preprogrammed or are we being continuously updated to the next major release?  
- Is current AI the ape-wise equivalent for some superintelligent AI ancestor? 
- Do we develop AI with a specific gender? Will AI choose to retain such a construct when it surpasses our own intelligence? 
- How do we know we haven’t already developed an AI that is smarter than us? It could simply fake it on the test. 
- Lightning strikes planes because we defy God. Our Promethean fate. 
- Through childhood we see life clean and bright. But as we get older, we lose touch with the fundamental excitement of the future. 
- The world was fundamentally darker for thousands of years. Then light bulbs come along. Why do illuminate that which happens in darkness? Some things are best left behind the curtain. 
- And in my blood, all those that came before. What fields were plowed? Dark skies glimpsed? Legacy carried onward? From somewhere we come. 
- The height of creative genius springs from raw self-managed ambition. To carve thine name in castle stone. Utter commitment and unfiltered sentimentality. 
- Human innovation simply requires opportune ingenuity
- There's nothing new in human experience 
- What ambitions reside in the planets above?
- The stars provide a mere curtain for the darkness beyond
- Drinking sparkling water is like drinking TV static
- The last clear footfalls from a high hung heaven
- A single tree dressed in Christmas lights in the middle of a meadow
- Picking grapes beneath a full moon with lantern in hand. Frosted footsteps upon the ground.
- Dark woods and tall trees in the south of France. Small homes and the crackling of encircled flames throwing shadows to the treetops. The epochs of man trudging the brown dirt of freshly plowed fields. Their toils amongst the fog soaked hills. It feels like I've been here before; at some point or another.
- The sheepskin cloth lain across the ground in front of the hearth. Fire crackles from the logs alight. In the near-dark wolves howl and the sky shines with starlight. Trees shake in the wind. They're restless. 
- We spend our entire life on an upwards trajectory. Climbing the rungs which lead heavenwards. 
- You can't give what you don't have
- I once won a great victory at the age of sixteen and thought I'd live to be a hundred. Now I know I shan't live to be thirty. 
- You remember the smallest things about big experiences.
- A suitcase that's always packed. Ready to go at a moment's notice. 
- Part of me wants to just disappear. Live on a beach in the Florida Keys. Build a boat. Sail the Gulf. Dive for mussels. 
- Last summer. Things I remember about last summer. 
- I want to fly into LA, rent a Jeep, and drive around the Hollywood Hills.
- Salespeople arise when several competing options exist. 
- For those who romanticize living off the land in small communities, that place already exists, its called Africa. 
- I don't like parties. Hard to get past the small talk and have deep conversation. 
- Drugs define the culture. Marijuana and beatniks. LSD and hippies. Coke and bankers. Ketamine and TikTok. 
- Dream. Peasants drinking and dancing in a cemetery. 
- Where the road ends, but the railroad tracks continue. 
- The morning commute. Sunlight spilling over the eastern horizon and your rearview mirrors ablaze like golden coins. Day's first promise. 
- The first 5 minutes of dawn 
- A flashlight in a pitch Black Forest 
- There's a bar in Cambodia where the liquor is free and you pay by the round of ammunition 
- You used to be able to dance here 
- Mountain skies are capable of a whole new color palette 
- Native Americans worshipping the night sky. A meteorite crashing in the desert. 
- People don’t like naturally attractive humans. They want someone who so clean, so groomed, so physically fit that they can almost believe that’s them somewhere down the road. 
- Sitting across from him and staring into the flames was his father. Each summer they came to this place ten thousand feet above the ground and hiked and camped and fished this place high in the mountains. 
- There’s nothin like a beer buzz. A warm Saturday, blue skied, brats on the grill, kinda beer buzz. 
- Can you imagine being a warlord in front of an army giving a rallying speech?
- A female prophet touching the face of stranger. Tattoos. Camped under a rock
- When you were more “in the moment” as a child, your parents were lost in yours 
- Great stories are as old as civilizations themselves
- We’ve become so fixated on tomorrow’s promise — fast cars and bright lights — that we’ve forgotten to stop and stare. Rest and relax. Look up and casually wonder.  
- The slaves beneath the city of Carthage.
- Off-ramp to infinity. 
- A doorway to the heavens. 
- An archway in the desert. Slingshot to the heavens. Portal to the stars. 
- What if you actually locked your diet in? Lost 20lbs. Dressed better. Went out more? We need to self actualize boys. Fortune favors the bold. 
- There’s something to be said about living in the shadow of a mountain. 
- The Acropolis 
- It’s like life is testing you. This is what you asked for. Is this what you really wanted?
- Parisian culture
- Bars were culture of the 90s. 
- Mom’s side was so stoic. They do not take to new trends. 
- I’m able to JUSTIFY my emotions
- I see a path to the top
- I saw myself wanting something. So I went and did it. Does that make me a prophet**?**
- Prior to literature, the only way to tell stories was by word of mouth. Reading the Greeks is reading the stories that were told to one another across dinner tables for centuries. They’re the stories that lasted. The first to be written down. 
- You remember the smallest things about big experiences.
- Dream. Standing in a mountain stream 
- First came the railroad tracks through mountain canyons. Dynamite and grit.
- The moments when we’re present. That’s when life feels infinite. 
- A sky undisturbed by jet trails 
- Every argument between two disagreeing factions will always simmer down whether god exists 
- Do the things you’re afraid of. Fear is just bullshit in your head. 
- Meteors bursting through the clouds, leaving holes in their white plumes, and crashing in the desert. Components of lost worlds now suddenly within our reach.  
- Most people fold under immense pressure. Others focus. 
- Everything slows down when it rains. 
- The process of going out to the river. Waders. That first cast. Reeling in and peeking the sky just beyond the tree line. 
- The human species only considers itself evolved based on its own discoveries. We use language and mathematics to describe a universe which is defined by simplicities. We’ve over-evolved. 
- AGI. Computation versus consciousness
- As children, we were prone to awe and wonder. Then we got older and peaked behind the curtain.
- Being in the moment. Watching the birds crawl the sky. The sound of the river. Mountains in the distance. Laughing with your best friend. 
- Architecture is the remnant of an era, of a civilization which built big. Which constructed with stone. Bronze. Steel. Which emphasized for the present such that it could last for the future. 
- Watching the wind get away from you
- Just barely closing your eyes. The sun’s glare a white sheen. 
- There are mountains of cloud overhead; peaks only visible from a distance. 
- Taking the back roads home. Miles of curtained sky overhead. 
- The whish of air as you blow out a candle 
- I found something today. I’ve been looking for a while now and finally found it. 
- Looking up to see reality scrawled backwards across the ceiling. 
- I looked up and lost myself. 
- I was asked where I’m from and I didn’t quite remember. 
- Feeding trending news into ChatGPT and having it generate content for you. Posting on Instagram. Recursive organic marketing 
- The more you read the more empathetic you become 
- The older you get, the less appealing “growth mindset” becomes. Savoring of simplicity. Enjoying day to day beauty.
- Gen Z is a chilled out millennial 
- Everything I see, I take it 10 steps further in my head 
- Actors are great at pretending no one is watching them 
- Every guy that peaked in high school is playing the new NCAA game 
- You see a little bit of the person you want to be in every potential partner
- California was the false future for the past generation 
- Stop over correcting your language when you write. The missing words make it read better anyway. That’s how you’d say it, why not write it. 
- I’m the kind of guy, you say you thought of something today and I already thought about it. 
- Stop overengineering the problem.
- The body language of a good model should bleed through the screen. Subtly evocative. 
- 80 degrees, humid as all hell, and you’re just making things worse in that tank top. 
- After being stuck at my computer all day, I stepped outside to rediscover the beauty of the real world. 
- The process of standing up a skyscraper 
- That truck stop in the middle of nowhere 
- Ascetic album covers 
- You have a very strong inner monologue. And it’s visible to others 
- It’s always the girls with tats and emotional damage that like me
- Why are there so many short Catholic guys? It’s like they enjoy being told what to do
- Waiting for the tea pot to settle in the late night 
- Depression is just being hung up on your thoughts 
- Every small town started along the river or the train tracks. 
- Waiting for the radio to play the right song 
- If Kings are living gods, does the reverse hold true? Are gods deified kings?
- Someone planted little pine trees on the rooftop of an apartment building. A headstart as they grope for the heavens. 
- Listen to your body. Give it more of what it wants. 
- You control your sense of time
- The idiomatic way you use ChatGPT - similar to the “art” of finding what you want on Google 
- The long term effects of young love 
- Live on the cusp 
- Medieval people were better suited for what lies ahead. Belief in a higher power 
- The Greeks knew being ostracized and put to death were the same thing 
- All new ideas are heretical
- Cicero fish ponds 
- The two pronged approach to computer vision 
- The sheer resources being thrown at AI
- It's like sticking your head out the window and seeing the whole world. 
- Debanking. The actual effects of 2009
- Take gravity to its logical conclusion 
- Riding the vibe. Translating that to art.
- What other mediums can you use for your ideas. Twitter? 
- Catching a vibe
- You gotta go somewhere new
- I love the wins bro. Good ideas and executing em. 
- I’m thinking too high level sometimes. 
- Your EQ is more useful than your IQ. 
- It starts as a social app. Meet and talk to someone new. It can be anyone in the world. But 1/5 new people are people from your town. Eventually you’ll be given the opportunity to ask the other person where they’re from. Maybe she’s the one?
- Book a couple weeks somewhere. Give an open invite to the homies 
- The goal of your life is to be as happy as possible. In all ways. The most sage find joy in everything. 
- Everybody has their own way of overthinking. 
- Exploring new art forms. Photography. Architecture. Adrenaline rushes. Getting lost. 
- European culture. The simplicity. The elegance. The cerebral. Budapest is quintessential. 
- You cannot be both commercial and artistic
- Shouting down from your bedroom window. And its well past midnight.
- Nothing attracts a Catholic girl like a sinner

name: A Mountainoues People.md
text:
## A Mountainous People

On the eastern slopes of the mountains, there are rivers that rush steadfast to the plains below. In the time before civilized man, villages began to take shape along the riverbanks of these waterways. As time progressed, and these tribes of men and women began to raise questions of God and places therein, these groups of people dissolved into the mists of the mountains.

Years later, as men came about and settled the land with their machines and earthly ambitions, the Land rejoiced, for men basked in its eternal beauty yet again. That is all the Land ever wanted — to be reveled in.

name: A Warm and Welcoming Beach.md
text:
## A Warm and Welcoming Beach

There is a beach somewhere. Its probably like every other beach that you have seen and possibly envisioned, but when you stop and stare at the waves lapping the shore you feel something. It isn't longing for that cool and seamless water on your skin. It isn't even the hot sun on your untanned skin. It's the endless water before you. It's the promise of freedom. And as you you unfurl the tight grip of stress that holds you immobile, your whisk away to somewhere you've only dreamed about.

name: Beauty Lost. Beauty Found.md
text:
## Beauty Lost. Beauty Found

The hills went rolling by on the other side of the frosted window pane, blur after blur entering and leaving his consciousness. Every few seconds he would focus on a new point, some distant mound of dirt, until it went rushing beyond his vison and out of his life forever. Carl had started in Dallas and caught a Greyhound to Tulsa. From Tulsa, in this very train, he had taken up homage for two straight days. There wasn't much to do on the train, of course there were multiple ladies whom had thrown a coquette glance his way, but beyond the objective form of glamour in the twinkle of of their eyes, he saw nothing but a means to the same end. He had left Dallas under the same narrative, a girl who left him empty...broken.

Of course, only a few months ago things seemed to be going great. Sarah had told her parents of their recent engagement and their plans of leaving that Midwestern town for a more chic destination. Paris perhaps? Or maybe the Riviera? The European lifestyle certainly seemed to suit those who were capable of leaving worldly ambitions behind. And it wasn't that they disliked Dallas so much as it was the nature of the people they spent their time with. Life had become so dull in the span of only a couple of years of serious dating. Suddenly, it became talk of houses that would suit a 'growing family' and weekends spent at the local dive bar of 37th street (three tumblers a piece, no more). And as of late that lifestyle became the substance that fueled many an argument. While they both could agree that they didn't want the life others became so quickly accustom to, they couldn't necessarily agree what exactly suited there particular taste for semi-domestic daring. Carl had brought up an extended honeymoon, one with no set return date. Backpacking Europe had become quite the rave as of late and would leave them both as satisfied travel bugs, but Sarah wanted more. She wanted to live, not just experience. To engage, not just see and touch. However, as Carl attempted to grow closer and closer to this dream that had become her cloak of identity, she merely shied from his pursuit, eventually shrugging his touch. Ultimately culminating in an abrasive, yet hopeful relationship. There was always tomorrow.

Carl brought his thoughts back to the present. The now. He had felt himself slipping farther into the depths of his love the farther he wandered away from that which emblazoned it so.

"Can I get you anything sir?" asked the waitress who was stooping towards him from the center aisle.

"A bottle of water would be great ma'am."

"Of course. Here you go." as she handed him the plastic bottle. "Hope you enjoy the remainder of your trip." Carl found this last sentiment quite amusing, not because she said it sardonically, but because he couldn't find one thing to enjoy in that moment in time, his happiness remained affixed to girl who had left him behind.

A couple of hours later, the train arrived in Casper, Wyoming. Carl's final destination. Grabbing his bags from the rear train car, he made his way across the street to purchase a truck for rental. Picking out a late model Ford, he paid with his credit card and loaded the truck bed, setting off north through town to his hotel.

Along the streets that line the main drag of Casper there are blue lights that hang from the street lamps. Surprisingly, these lights don't signify anything in particular. They were simply hung by a local drug store owner during the years following the depression to allow coal miners working the night shift to make their way back home in the early hours of the morning; before the new day's dawn cast down it's infant beginnings. The blue light seemed to hum and dance in the early evening air as he continued through town.

Pulling up to a stop sign, a young girl made her way across the street in front of him. Wearing a baby blue Columbia jacket and ankle high red rubber boots, she passed by without casting him a glance. But as she came upon the opposite side of the street, she suddenly leapt near the drainage entrance and sent water bursting in all directions. The girl suddenly turned about and looked Carl head on, whether she saw him through the tinted truck glass he was unsure, and her face was filled from ear to ear with an ecstatic grin. Carl found himself grinning in response, wanting to commend the child for her momentous watery leap, but before he had the change to roll down the window, she was gone. A mass of baby blue on its way to find another form of giddy amusement.

Continuing on, Carl made his way to the north side of town and found his Motel 8 nearly deserted, only a dirt covered Jeep in the lot. Parking his truck, he grabbed his things from the back and made his way inside. At the front desk, there was no one there, only a bell to ring for assistance. Carl set down his things, rolled his sore shoulders a couple times, and rang the bell. A couple seconds later a middle aged woman, with large round glasses that enlarged her brown eyes, came around a corner behind the desk.

"What will it be sir?" she said.

"I have a room reserved under Carl Cramer." he replied. "I called in yesterday and double checked with someone here."

"Yep, I see ya here. Just one sec while I get you checked in."

Carl set his things down at his side and let his eyes traverse the room. There was a small fireplace and sitting area directly behind him, opposite of the front desk. No one was sitting there, but he noticed a still burning cigarette in the ashtray atop the table adjacent one of the recliners. Wisps of smoke rose lazily through the air, becoming fainter the farther it rose.

name: Daily Flow.md
text:
### Daily Flow

He'd come over for dinner but had already eaten so we settled for cigars. 

You ever been over to Nina's on Western Ave? 

Sure, go there to read sometimes. Sometimes I prep for the LSAT there too. Lots of young people. Good place to spend a few hours on a Saturday morning. 

I think their are a lot of folks their posing. Maybe even ourselves. Leaving the apartment to read a book somewhere louder but filled with fresh faces. We probably spend more time convalescing then we do in a state of productivity. 

Sure. Isn't that why coffee shops exist in the first place? 

I don't know. 

Why do you care if someone else is their for a reason beyond their self-presumed means? 

I guess it feels like we're all just kidding ourselves. And I hate that bullshit. No one can be real with one another anymore. 

I think you need to get out more. 

I think I need to get out less. 

Do you see yourself as a hermit? Intellectual acolyte? 

I don't see myself as anything. I guess I just want to understand the truth of it all. 

Let me tell you something. No one knows what the fuck they are talkin about. Everyone has notions in their head that they've struck as scripture in the tomes of their heart. It makes them feel better. A lot of times they don't even know what's written on the stones. They'd rather not know. 

So you don't chase dragons? 

What the fuck does that mean? 

I don't know. Guess it means drive. Gumption. Aspirations. 

So just cause I don't want to be a doctor, that means I don't have goals? 

That's not what I'm saying. 

Sure as shit sounds like what you are saying. 

Where do you draw the line? When do you lay stake and accept what's coming? Why do people do that at all? 

I don't know. Sometimes its easier that way. 

Exactly. 

Exactly what? 

Nevermind. Let's pay and get out of here.

name: Darkened Snow Showers.md
text:
## Darkened Snow Showers

Fourteen days had passed. Fourteen long and gloomy days; darkened by an onset of snow showers that fell seemingly without end. The darkness hung limpid outside my bedroom window, as if hung unbeknownst from a heaven above.

_Fifteen days ago._

As we walked through the boroughs of the city, I remained particularly amazed at the distinct qualities of the squarish suburban houses that condensed the street on either side. There were browns and blues and a mix of everything in between that left me thinking that every home owner upon this street must take great pride in rendering a structure of obsolete qualities. However, this thought drifted out of immediate reach when I felt his hand squeeze mine yet again. I glanced over to gauge his reaction, expecting to see a hint of a smile hover on his lips, but was not too surprised to see his attention remained affixed upon the jaunty houses so predominantly laid before us. I found it amusing, yet mildly intriguing, that his large hands seemed to flicker and pulse without reason when coupled with mine for extended periods of time; as if he had let slip that controlled firmness of his character that he imposes upon our initial interactions.

While this was not our first time taking such a walk, it certainly wasn't a commonality among us, and I was starting to detect the opening of that warm masculine heart: one reverberating beat at a time. His name was Amory, which he told me was inherited from a great-grandfather of a bygone era. To me, it was Amory, mortal depiction of a rapturous and fleeting _amor_. We had gotten coffee for the first time a couple weeks ago, which is considered now-a-days to be the quintessential first date - which I would probably have to agree with. Short enough of an encounter to allow for a quick escape if need be, and the ability to be elongated given the proper tone of conversation and the excessive batting of eyelashes. In this very place and time, my heart was being filled ever so quickly via a sieve of thoughtful remarks and meaningful glances, seemingly without end.

_Today_.

I'm not sure what came to pass during these past two weeks, for that was the last time I saw Amory, and my days have drifted into a blurry re-enactment of a previous life; one where I walk alone among the happily invested beings of this world. For as I peer through the fogged glass window, I can see the snow cascading to the ground in lazy fashion. Couples walk by with lack of haste, as if they had already attained their destinations within their clasped hands. As I step away from the window I softly caress the inside of my left hand with my right's fingers, hopelessly sentimental for days and lovers past. Winter had arrived, and depravity of love along with it.

name: Dirt Road Feeling.md
text:
## Dirt Road Feeling

Do you ever find yourself looking down, not up?

It's a scorching summer twilight in southern Kansas (at least for someone who is not used to these kinds of temperatures) and there are miles of dirt road stretched out in front of me. My car is somewhere behind me, out of my periphery, and it isn't the yellow horizon or quaint rural farms that draw my gaze - it's the rough brown gravel beneath my feet. It's the sound the earth makes when I traipse over it, headed nowhere in particular. It's the feeling of lying somewhere in-between the heavens and the tomblike quality of everyday American life. I am suspended.

name: Downward Gaze. Horses Below.md
text:
## Downward Gaze. Horses Below.

Sometimes, if you adamantly focus, you can still see the horses pounding across the valley below. The golds and browns and blacks of their manes splay dramatically across your vision until you have to tilt back your head and focus your vision against a pale blue dawn; but when you look back, all those pretty horses are gone.

I like to imagine cases like these, myself atop a series of switchbacks on the backside of some long forgotten mountain and beautiful things below me - taking no mind that I infringe upon their ethereal existence.

name: Driving Somewhere.md
text:
### Driving Somewhere

"Hey Don, want to grab a beer?" says Pete. Pete loves beer, but who doesn't? 

"Sure, wanna just head to Norton's?" I reply. Pete contemplates this for a moment, weighing the importance of it all. "Naw man, Bricktown is the place tonight. Girls drink free, you know what that means." He's right. There is one thing that Pete sure knows a lot about, and that's girls. 

"I can drive man," I announce to the group. We all hop in the car and start heading down to Central. I let Pete throw his IPod on since I know I'll have to hear all about my bad taste in music if I decide to run the tunes. 

"Do you really think Maddy and Holden are hookin' up? It doesn't add up to me. In terms of height that is," Dave says and we all laugh. "Who knows D, maybe he grew a few inches since graduation." I can't help but think that there are hundreds, probably thousands, of more interesting topics than the fellow graduation class and their respective faults. Meanwhile, the palm trees are sliding by on each side -- contributing to the quote-on-quote 'California daze' we all seem to be experiencing since arriving in Malibou last weekend.

name: Faint Golden Glow.md
text:
## A Faint Golden Glow

Assuredly, he walked out the entrance of the gym and into the cool summer night that awaited him. As he took his earbuds out and let the gentle breeze and revving engines fill his senses, he let his gaze wander to the night's wandering clouds. To the west, where the populous city beckoned and disgusted with equal force, he could see the subtle glow of the city's artificial lights painting a golden hue on the undersides of the heavens. He paused in the empty parking lot near his car and simply tried his best to take it all in; everything that was present and everything that was missing, in that moment in time, he felt slipping from his grasp — never to be fully attained.

name: Finding the Angle.md
text:
## Finding the Angle 

The man remembered the maple trees that lined his parent’s driveway in autumn. Clouds that looked like jet fumes hung in the afternoon sky and the wind spun the leaves in rustling fits. Laying back in the cold grass he’d contemplate the world’s geometry; denoting the angle upon which his ambitions could rocket to the stars.

name: Firenze.md
text:
## Firenze

In Firenze there is a small row of shops that make up your right and left as you make your way first through. Bricklayers had put grindstone to rock and carved stones of varying but precise sizes in millennia past that still make up the streets which your feet currently press down upon. The shops allow slots of sunlight to make their way though the gaps between them and your figure casts an oblong shadow in that sunlight and on those stones as you pass from one building to next. There are red and white picnic blankets laid across tables and bottles of wine resting in their center. Conversations float softly in air. Perhaps the quotations of fine literature or the re-administration of words ordained and cast forthwith but oft retold as be said to suspected capable only the Gods. You reach the last shop and enter through wooden doors with plate glass inlaid and make your way through an empty room sparingly filled with tables and chairs and reaching the back door step out onto a wide patio that sits atop a steep outcropping that looks down upon the city. You can see water below as it widens and splits the town in two. You breath. First in, then out. Firenze.

name: Floating.md
text:
## Floating

The dark treeline bespoke a world much darker than expected. He calmed his nerves with the flask in his right hand, feeling the Coriolis effect gently pushing his soul skywards. No longer grounded. Free floating somewhere between heaven and Earth. Finally free. He heard whippoorwills calling from the deep. Inflectionless. A harp-tone played only for him. 



the pull of the Coriolis 
from the turnings of the world

the calling of the whippoorwills 
from a darkened treeline 

and a full moon
           from above
like a flashlight

name: Gatsby & Nick - Green Light Scene.md
text:
## Gatsby & Nick - Green Light Scene

I followed Gatsby out through the swining back doors that lead to the stone portico. He paused on the top step for a moment, staring out across the dark waters.

"Did you ask Daisy about tomorrow's luncheon Old Sport?" he asked, without dropping his gaze.

"I did, I did. She sounded delighted." I replied. "But Jay, if I may ask, why don't you just ask her yourself?"

Seconds seemingly became hours as I waited for Gatsby to respond, only the sounds of the empty darkness filling the silence. "Daisy can't know I will be coming Old Sport, this is essential! It would ruin everything. . . it would ruin everything." I let his words sink in, sensing the imperativeness that came along with them.

name: Going Out With Her Family.md
text:
## Going Out With Her Family

"What time did you tell them we'd be there?" I asked her upon getting home from work. It had been a long day at work and I didn't really want to go out tonight but we'd made plans a couple weeks ago with Natalie's sister Melissa and her husband Nick. One of those committments you make when in a candid situation that makes it impossible to say no without being rude. 

"6pm babe! Did you remember to pick up the wine like I asked you too?" she replied from the bathroom. 

"Yep," I responded while taking my shoes off in the entryway. "I grabbed a white and red, figured we could bring both and let them decide?" I hadn't much experience with wine and could honestly have cared less what we drank. A drink did sound good right now though. Something strong to take the edge off. 

"Babe! You can't bring _two_ wines..." cried Nat from the bathroom. She stepped out at this point and I asked why not. "Because you _just don't_!" she responded rather sharply, stepping back into the bathroom. I could see her touching up her eyeliner - giving the mirror that wide eyed stare and blinkly rapidly, moving her head from side to side. 

"Alright, let's bring the white. That ought to do it." I sat down in the living room, putting my feet up and letting out a sigh, the kind of one that usually follows a long day and long commute home. "How was your day off?" I asked Nat while turning the TV on. Nat was a nurse at Fairview. Emergency room. Seemed like everyone in medical had weird work schedules. Sometimes weekdays. Others weekends. Friday nights? I mean, come on, how do you do date nights when your significant other is working every other Friday night. Makes it hard to keep the romance going really. And going out to a romantic dinner on a Tuesday doesn't have the same appeal. There's work the next day (for one of you that is) and you've got work on the brain. But whatever, you make the most of it, like agreeing to have dinner with your girlfriend's sister and husband on a night when you'd much rather stay in a watch a movie. Maybe give Nat a massage. You know where that usually leads. Mental note, give Nat a massage when you get home tonight.

Nat stepped out of the bathroom and came over to the couch, bending down to give you a kiss. "Good to see you babe," she said. ""How was your day?" 

"Pretty good. Kinda slow to be honest. Paul needed some help this afternoon so spent most of my time helping him. Didn't get a lot done as a result." I replied, trying to make eye contact intermittnely and also follow the news flashing on the TV. "That's good," she replies and heads back to the bathroom to finish getting ready. 

Shortly thereafter you and Nat get into your car and head over to her sister's house where Nick opens the door to greet you. "Hey," he says enthusiastically. You like Nick. Decent guy, doesn't bullshit you. "How are you guys doing?"

"Good," we both reply more or less at the same time.

name: Harrowing Headstones.md
text:
- Political Satire, Cemetery, Coloring Book

Brainstorm:

- Cemetery caretaker arrives to group of people standing in anger over a series of graffitied headstones
- In anger, he begins to rant about the uncouthness of today's generation
- The people begin to nod and shout in agreement
- Folks in crowd begin adding in things that are wrong about todays society
- Internally, he doesn't necessarily agree, but nods his agreement all the same
- Fast forward a few months and we find the caretaker speaking at a rally for his campaign for political office
- As he leaves the stage, a young girl asks for his autograph and hands him a coloring book, an image of himself is crudely sketched inside
- Moment of self reflection or sudden end of story

## Harrowing Headstones

An everyday caretaker becomes the martyr the city thinks it needs - only to be reminded that he’s not.

A lengthy shadow was laid out before me. Not a morbid kind of shadow, but rather the kind of shadow that was cast from an early morning’s golden hour - as if the light was founded from a planet afar or heaven above. I let this light foster for a moment before carrying on down the sidewalk. I found it odd, yet not unbelievable, that I had been making this morning commute for 30 years. It was as if time was incomprehensible, a thing for vague understanding. I stopped at the crosswalk and watched as a garbage truck rumbled by. The crossing indicator soon illuminated and I made my way across the two way street, letting the sound of the city permeate my senses while I gently reminisced.

For nearly my entire life, I have been the caretaker of Blessed Following cemetery in west St. Paul, which most would assume isn’t a full time job. However, what most don’t understand is that there are incredible amounts of work that go into maintaining a cemetery if one does it _properly_. The seasonal responsibilities revolve around mowing the grass and spraying pesticides in the summer while snow removal and tree trimming in the winter months. On top of all this, a caretaker is also responsible for marking out burial plots and coordinating plot maintenance with families when necessary - for you see, many folks don’t feel remorse for lost loved ones until after they are gone and find themselves bringing flowers and paying for headstone maintenance to serve as sinful reparations. All of which I am in certain agreeal with - a clean and well kept grave is the key to honoring one’s memory.

While being the sole proprietor of a cemetery can be a lonely existence, I rather prefer it. There is only one person’s opinion of which I am concerned with and that is my own (and of the families whose loved ones reside in my cemetery of course). Never do I have to bother with incessant coworkers or an overbearing manager. I am, and always will be, completely independent of other’s opinions. And this, along with the beauty of my well kept burial ground, is what makes my job worth doing.

Picking up my step a bit, I cross West 7th, which is the last street I have to cross before arriving at Blessed Following. But there was something rather odd about this morning’s distant view of my destination, for there was a large gathering of people out in front of the cemetery’s front gates. As I grew nearer I heard the group of people conversing in charged tones, anger inflecting the periphery.

“This CANNOT stand any longer!” cried a large woman who had her back presently turned to me. “This is the second time this week our neighbourhood has been vandalized.” Worry began to creep within me. Almost sprinting now, I pushed past the crowd and looked inwards at the holy ground that was my life's work. Graffiti. Spread across almost every headstone in the first row closest to the entrance. **FUCK 12** was spraypainted in pink across more than one headstone. **DOWN WIT THE PATRIARCHY** across others. Rage was bubbling up from somewhere long forgotten, a deep maroon filled my senses.

“What happened to the respect of man and his property!” I cried with alacrity. “Does no one realize the state of this country and its inhabitants? Where are the parents? Where have they been for the past 40 years and why aren’t they here right now?!” There were nods of agreement from the crowd. “Something needs to be done and it needs to be done now” I growled with resolve. Suddenly an older gentleman stepped forward and clapped me on the shoulder, turning to face the crowd by my side. “This man has got it right, we do need change, and we need it right now!” he called out to the ever growing crowd. “The entire system needs to be torn down, root and stem. And this man right here is the one to do it!” With this, the crowd erupted in cheers. “What’s your name, friend?” the gentleman asked as the crowd continued to cheer. “James Jaquer” I replied, “but I don’t know if I’m really suited for public office...” I never got to finish the thought as the gentleman proceeded to engage the crowd and chant “JAQUER! JAQUER! JAQUER!”

_Six months later_

“And that is why I am the best candidate for this job” I cried out to the crowd laid out before me. “You there, young man in the blue shirt. What can I do for you?” The assistant nearest the man handed him a microphone. “Well sir,” he began with confidence, “there is a cemetery adjacent to my business which continually uses pesticides to treat the property for weeds. The smell wafts over and displeases my customers, but the caretaker refuses to halt his pesticide usage. I ask that you enact a city ordinance to disallow such actions!” The crowd murmured with agreement. “It would be my honor to propose such legislation as my first act as your new mayor!” I shouted to the masses as they surged from their seats with cheers.

After throwing a fist skyward in an act of hopeful triumph, I left the stage, meeting my campaign manager at the bottom of the steps who had a young girl standing next to him. “This is Ariel” he said, “and she would very much like an autograph from our soon-to-be elected mayor.” I crouched to be eye level with the girl. “Of course, it would be my pleasure.” The girl offered me a small coloring book with minute flowers on the cover. As I opened the book to the first page to sign, I was alarmed to see a rough sketch of a man holding a “Elect Jaquer” sign in the midst of grey cubes. “What are these exactly?” I asked with genuine interest. “Those are gravestones from your cemetery Mr. Mayor.”

name: He's Leaving.md
text:
## He's Leaving

"Where is he going?"

"He said a few weeks ago that he was going to Argentina."

"Does he know anyone there?"

"Not that I know of."

"He's just quitting his job and moving to Argentina?"

"Yep."

"What's he gonna do?"

"Said he's gonna find himself."

"What does that mean?"

"I don't know."

"You know yourself?"

"Reckon so."

"I thought I knew him."

"Who?"

"Your roommate that just left for Argentina."

"Oh yeah. Him."

"One day you step out from the office building and the sun is shining in your eyes. The next you disappear. Don't tell anyone where you're going. You need some time. We all understand. We wish we could go with you. Maybe someday when the kids are older -- when the mortgage is paid off."

"We should wave goodbye."

"Okay."

name: Hope and Prayer.md
text:
### Hope and Prayer

I'd stopped for a beer, something to ease the edge from white knuckling the steering wheel for hours on end - trying not to think about it. About her. About whatever shit kept leaping headlong into my brain. HI THERE!

Anything else hun? Why no - this is precisely what I need. The door rang open. A sleigh bell. A promise. Dont get me fucking started on promises. 

The car had New Hampshire plates, an eternal grain of road kill stuck to the enamel, and cheap despaire oozing through the cracked windows in curlicue formation. You could smell the hash thirty feet away yet you couldnt help but be attracted. Destinations. Cheap liquor. Short meals and tight stomachs. 

I tried not to make eye contact but you know the type. You plannin on drinking that 6-pack yourself? Hell no, just figured I'd start with one and let God drive for a change. 

Seems sort of fucked to me, he lets his son get crucified but suddenly you're worth saving? She had a point. 

I tried not to look. I mean I seriously did. But she had legs - the long tan kind that invite your eyes, make you wander towards the stars. 

You've got a point, where you headed? Somewhere warm and somehwhere that is far away from here she said. Heard you can forget if you drive long enough. Seems like a theory worth testing. 

There were long shadows creeping up the parking lot by this point. The sound of the other cars pulling out of the lot and a soft cadence of the whipoorwills could be heard over the dull hum coming from the interstate. Something in me wanted to go and get lost. Find a corner lot in Palm Springs and wring out for a while. Disappear. 

You got any cash? Never realized how expensive gas was till you look in your wallet and realize there aint much left. 

Yeah, I responded. How much you need? 

A twenty would be great. 

I handed her two tens. 

I'll be right back if you're planning on sticking around. She said it with bright eyes. And tan legs. Im not ashamed to say I watched her walk away. Partly out of appreciation, but mostly contemplation. Or fuck, maybe its condemnation.

_there were boughs, they extended from above like relections from cold dark stars  ... she said she wouldnt stay should have believed her ... should help her load the car ...shouldnt be lifting such boxes in her condition I told doctor "she wont listen goddamn it"_ ...

I drove away. Partly out of cowardice and partly because I didn't trust myself. So much there to remember and too much to forget.

name: MSP --> LHR.md
text:
## MSP --> LHR

I took a flight from Minneapolis to London. Had done it before, this flight. First time at night though. Spent most of the time time eating, sleeping, dreaming. But I woke up round midnight and the rest of the plane was asleep. Slid the window cover open and took a look for myself at the ocean. There were clouds casted in a thin mold between me and the darkness below and I imagined myself falling. The stars mere lamplight as I descended. There must have been sattelites for I saw them as beacons across the cosmos and almost considered returning. To you, that is. But instead I'll just sit here, at cruising altitude. Maybe order another whiskey and drift away for awhile. But I'll be back. Promise you won't forget me. I loved you in my dreams.

name: Moment of Alacrity.md
text:
## Moment of Alacrity

Like all structures that broach the heavens, it is best to come on foot. To let the infinitesimal surroundings fall back in a blur as your perspective becomes dominated by what is before you. Your pace slows, alacrity fills your soul, and time is inconceivable. Here and now: you are.

Slowly, the omnipresent settles back in. Figures become starkly outlined - catching and holding your gaze for moments on end. And while you pause and wait for that feeling to return, that feeling indescribeable, you grow sad. You look skyward, vainly hoping for something similar to fixate your wordly inertia, but eventually start on — eyes and heart held gently in place.

name: New York A Following.md
text:
## New York: A Following

The towering skyscrapers of downtown Manhattan serve as a sort of veil to all the damned souls below their canopy. Mere insects to the folks in the upper stories. Yet, these obelisks have a certain appeal. They serve as a focus point for the monetary ambitions of so many human souls that it's surprising we don't have a festival each year in their names. And it was here, along First Avenue, that my Oxford soles tread.

 It was not unusual for me to walk those paved streets with no sense of direction. I had been working for two months at Powell & Lowe as a broker (fresh out of college) and had yet to have a day where I left my apartment with alacrity. As my steps lead me onward I notice to my left among the alcove that was a French bistro a single man sitting at a table capable of seating four. Thin rolls of smoke rise gently from the cigarette resting on the table and I note a half eaten pastry. He appeared to be looking vacantly across the street -- a look of intense reminisce on his face -- as if he could wrestle back some memory if only he tried a bit harder.

I paused for a moment, stepping closer to the street as to allow the other passerby's to skirt by. At that moment, the man which my attention was fixated seemed to snap from the past and proceeded to gather his thoughts; abruptly vacating the bistro. I watched from afar as he walked north along First Avenue. I wondered what thought had so firmly gripped his consciousness in that moment of quiet reflection? I wasn't surprised to later learn that it was the premonition of a love lost; the tragedy was oft to be found on his dark complexion. It was destined to be branded upon his soul. Such is the story of Don Drexler.

name: Northern AZ Existential.md
text:
## Northern AZ Existential

Don would spend his days at work. Eight hours each day on the blacktop painting new lines. Laying fresh asphalt. When he got off work he'd make his way back into Cottonwood from the jobsite and through the cul-de-sac and down the hill behind the newly framed house to the renovated garage that was his home. He'd shower, the drain running black with fainted tar as he scrubbed himself clean. Sometimes he'd turn the radio on and listen to something upbeat as he got dressed and grabbed a water bottle from the fridge, making his way back to the truck and heading north of town towards Sedona.

name: Novel - Existential Western.md
text:
## Novel - Existential Western

1. Working at tech company with intellectual driven girlfriend
2. Quits job, breaks up with girlfriend, and moves to Wyoming
3. Becomes accustom to new environment
4. Meets new girl. Seemingly simple and midwestern moralistic. Turns out to be intricate in unforeseen ways.
5. Ex-girlfriend calls. Existential crisis. Climax. Either awakening or sudden end.
6. Very short epilogue or abstract existential parallel.

I would like to think that there is something more than the endless circles of mediocrity that we find ourselves traversing day in and day out — but maybe that's me just being optimistic, possibly even romantic. But when life goes on, and the beginnings and ends of those circles become a blur of existential mediocrity, we remain caught. Trapped is a better word. Forever ensnared. Maybe that's why I left Laura and all the things that she made right in me. There was something better out there, and a life with her was merely choosing an easier way out that I was unwilling to cope with. And all this goes to show is that I am a seemingly ambitious man who fears the bonds of any sort. I am haunted today, tomorrow, and always.

name: Old Logging Trail.md
text:
## Old Logging Trail

It was an old logging trail. Likely used in the 1800s as a means of accessing the timber that resided at the base of the mountain. The man had been able to navigate his truck through the ruts of several miles but ultimately had to stop and park when he reached fallen lumber. He proceeded on foot. 

There were rumors that the trout and trees grew larger at the highest elevations of the mountain. To cradle a trout in hand and raise eyes to the tops of swaying pines trees as the mountain air swirls. There is no other feeling. 

Hours passed underfoot and elevation came with it. Looking around the man could see for miles and he contemplated the steadfast rushing of streams to their older and wiser river kin. Ponder the floating of a flower from the top of the mountain to some expanse of ocean hundreds of miles away. Where does one thing begin and another end?

I have a vivid memory of standing in a field as a child. Husks crunching underfoot and the hard yellow kernels cast awide as if from an atomic blast.

name: Pre-war Coffee Connection.md
text:
## Pre-war Coffee Connection

Before the war, we would meet at Jani's on Thursday morning's for coffee. We weren't 'together' per say, but if some stranger were to see us in that coffee shop on a Thursday morning, it would be hard to think otherwise. I never considered myself a coffee drinker prior to us starting this weekly routine, so I would try something different each week. Last week it was a white caramel mocha, this week it was an Americano with a drab of cream — I was attempting to work my way towards pure black diesel, but it was an uphill battle. I simply enjoyed sweet things far too much. Maybe that's why I bought her that first coffee — the one that started it all. She was just that sweet.

Most times you hear the guy recount how he saw the girl at a distance and watched her pass him by for months on end until finally manning up the courage to ask for her name or make some offhand remark, but this was different. I was standing in line, like every other Thursday for the last couple years, just waiting for my morning caffeine when she tripped over her own feet and right into the back of my coat. I can still hear her gasp as tumbled into me. However, she didn't fall. By using my as her steadying support, she got her feet back under her and began apologizing profusely. And while normally my manners would have kicked in and I would have apologized myself (for not doing anything of course), I didn't have any words. I just looked her in the eyes and was lost, I still am.

Eventually the sound of the coffee shop returned to my consciousness and I told her not to worry, but not before

name: Scene - Fire Tower.md
text:
## Scene: Fire Tower

Something to do with someone falling from a fire tower under a bruised sky.

name: Searching for Sunset.md
text:
## Searching for Sunset

He'd taken to walking the street at night. Night walks he called it. It was only a couple mile walk down to Sunset Boulevard and he'd take his time - trying to turn his brain off while doing so. It wasn't uncommon to run into the occasional peddler or vagrant stoop but he just kept his eyes lowered and continued on his way. There was this street sign on Moldova Ave -- when he was only a couple blocks from reaching Sunset -- that said "THAT FEELING" and he tried every time to consider what the advertisement meant. Or what it made him feel? He still didn't know, but sometimes the chorus of "Where the Streets Have No Name" would playback at the moment he made eye contact with the board and carry on until he took his first step onto Sunset. Then the melody would stop, the moon holding moot court with the stars in the sky above, and the leaves rustling in the ocean breeze. 

After enough time had passed, he'd make his way home, his footsteps lighter on the pavement. She'd oftentimes be waiting, knowing roundabouts when he'd be returning and seating herself on the porch while waiting. She liked to see him come up the hill towards the house they had shared for several years now. She knew he'd always find his way home eventually. She liked to think it was because of her, but she knew it what it really was - that thing that padded softly in the deep recesses of his soul. She just did her best to dampen it. 

"Hey," she said when he got close enough. "You wanna come to Daphne's with me? She said you could come if you wanted." 

"No thanks," he replied quietly. "I think I'll just grab a Perrier and sit out by the pool." He knew what Daphne really wanted - and why she had made a point to tell Bree that "he could come if he wanted". He'd given it to her this morning when he'd told Bree he was going for a jog. _I knew you'd come when called Dont give me any of that I swear i never told her Don't hurt me I swear Is this how you like to see me?_ 

"Alright, you can't get pissy with me tomorrow when I totally invited you Brad. Like, this is me inviting you. Okay?"

"Ya. Got it," he gave her a kiss on the cheek and headed for the fridge, grabbing an unopened Perrier from the fridge, slamming it shut, and taking his shirt off while heading out the back sliding door towards the pool. _You're a slut My Dad said he saw your Mom working Ever gotten off like this Daph?_ He fell down into a deeply recliner beach chair and took his first sip of the Perrier. It reminded him of the Champagne his Mom used to buy for them as kids. _Should come home more Proud of you Brad He fucked her He Did I saw it._ The sun was shining over the Beverley Hills and he thought it was August, but hadn't been paying attention lately.

name: Some Stranger.md
text:
## Some Stranger

"Do you know where the nearest bus stop is?" Jamie asked. She had a 6 month old wrapped in a pink blanket under one arm. "You lost or somethin lady? Don't see yur type round here much. Sure wish I did if ya know what I mean" the stranger let out a sharp shriek of laughter that sounded like a train slamming on the breaks. "Please sir, just tell me where the fucking bus station is. I just need to get away from here" Jamie replied exhasperatedly. "Sure, sure. I can take you where you need to go. You and yer lil one just follow me" and the shoddily dressed stranger started walking up the street.

name: The Forest.md
text:
## The Forest

Frost crunches underfoot as you snap the necks from blades of grass. Stopping a moment, you raise eyes to the surrounding of darkened woods. There are thinly boughed pine trees on all sides and in all directions. The full moon above casts an ethereal silver glow across all which can be seen and that which cannot. Enchanting shadows lay atop stolid ground and poetry whispers in the shaking of pine-needled branches.  

Its been over a year now -- since you came to France. Started in Paris as one does and spent your modest savings at the brothels on bottom-shelf plonk and cheap prostitutes. But you don't regret it, just needed to get that out of your system, after all. 

How you came to be here -- in the forest of Compiègne in the dead of night with nothing but the flycatchers and finches to keep you company -- is another thing entirely.

name: The Orchard.md
text:
## The Orchard

Under the boughs of orchard trees, against the intense reddening of the evening sky, laid a lank figure. Dressed in some dark color undistinguishable from the glowing solar backdrop in which he lay juxtaposed, one could not tell if he be a earthly acolyte or a local derelict cast downward for the same reasons.

Trucks rolled slowly down the newly rutted orchard rows, children standing in the beds grasping apples in hand and placing them delicately in the burlap sacks circling their feet. You can smell the vehicle's exhaust mingling with the spring scent of the trees - forcing the Eden daydreams from your forethoughts and reinstituting your early morning resolve. The same of which forced your bare legs outward from under the covers this morning, ready for the freshly minted day under the boughs of the orchard trees.

name: The Upward Glance.md
text:
## The Upward Glance

For years the priest had been amongst the most regarded of those at the sanctuary. The people of the nearby villages came in the early morning wrapped in furs and propositioned the questions of the world to him. For all he did not have answers. Most of all the questions of our mere existence. And each night the priest cast his knees to the floor and his heart to the heavens and raised these questions of its peoples. Not once a reply not once a thunderclap a hiss or a sigh. But as the clergyman lay his head down each night the beams of the Heavens refracted the Moon and he saw prophecies take shape. For some it was numbers, others literature. Yet, a common belief. In the upward glance.

name: They Never Left.md
text:
## They Never Left

When Gods are sent to earth they become demi-gods. Not only are they sent to Earth but they are thrown there, cast there, fall down from above. Yet here they stay and remain while they search for soul. The thing that makes us mortal. The stuff that fills your throat when you break your heart. When you stand in a wheat field as the sun alights your landscape with the glow of opportunity. With the warmth of tomorrow's promise. 

They’re still here. You never left.

name: Tomorrow.md
text:
## Tomorrow

Two men race along the hillside as the final hours of daylight shine from the horizon. A handful of cumulous clouds sail the airways. From their amphitheatrical height the crashing of waves can be heard from below. 

The men pause for a moment, catching their breath with hands behind head. The sun a shimmering carousel with golden aura sent spinning across the cosmos, observable only from this vantage. Tomorrow�s concerns are forgotten a moment as they refurnish their soul with what surrounds them. Bats and skylarks twist and turn in the wind. 

Ring forth a new day. This one is through.

name: Upon a Train.md
text:
## Upon a Train

There were rays of sunshine cutting through the window upon which my head rested and landing in diverse patterns on the floor. Sometimes, if I cast my eyes above the horizon, those same beams of heavenly light would blind me momentarily — leaving me in a sort of kaleidoscopic daze. And it wasn't that I enjoyed the lack of sight in that instant, rather that the colors spattering my vision invoked a warm sensation in my blood. Like sitting close to the fireplace on a cold night or getting wrapped in a hug by someone wearing a large sweater.

name: Utter Suppression.md
text:
## Utter Suppression

He stumbled along the gravel road, knees weak and buckling with each renewed step. How could he know that she hated him her fingers on his tracing the swirls how could he possibly know? More broken strides forward under that opalescent sky, a crushing sky, an endearing quality that sky had — forcing all mankind to an Atlas-like oppression wind blew a single curl in her hair upward and across her brow inching closer and closer to his heart this unutterable and unheroic sky. Forever he would remember this day, this sky, these boulders upon his shoulders. He finally collapsed onto all fours like the mangy whelp he was Father says you don't have the mind for this kind of business nor will he be able to teach you but its fine since we can move away and you can be a writer and I can be your inspiration inching forward with every haphazard jolt of movement he could muster Father said I never loved you and that you never loved me you just loved all I had to give finally collapsing on the ground in full mongrel suppression. There were no clouds that day, there never would be again. The clouds and stars and seemingly all pretty things that could not be contained or understood were there yet not — to never be again.

name: Wistful Coastal Glide.md
text:
## Wistful Coastal Glide

As we road along a coastal highway that mid-summer night, I could smell freshness in the air, cast upwards by the Mediterranean below. It wasn't the subtle salty wisp that catches you near the Atlantic coast -- no -- this was the stimulating aroma of blue waters and endless tranquility.

We road on.

Twisting and curving we went, regretting each bend that brought our vision landward and our souls ever closer to our esoteric earthly ambitions. However, when the driver wrought the steering wheel towards the sea, there was an assimilating of heart and mind, heaven and earth. The wind went rushing past our ears and the darkness enveloping the sea left the stars eternally drowning. We were somewhere, yet nowhere. Forever lost in that place and time.

name: a_higher_power.md
text:
He had left the States years ago, come to make home on the southern coast of France. He'd found an old stonemasons mill and asked the farmer with the homestead nearest if he could spend his days there. Seeing no reason why not, given the previous owner had come to pass decades before, the farmer obliged. The man paid 15 Francs for every month of residence and helped the farmer during spring planting and fall harvest.

As the years went on, the man spent less and less time amongst the people of the local community. Instead, opting to walk the shoreline of the Mediterranean. Rays would glide through the soft blue water. He'd wade towards them hoping to get a better look but they'd no care for the populous. Leaving his clothes on the rocks of the coast he'd dive and search for octopi - their eyes would meet his the moment before spearing them with his makeshift trident. Sometimes he'd stay there, on the coast. Walk naked to the rocky outcrop above the crashing waves and extend his fingertips to the throne of heaven as it slid in-and-out of view amongst the blinking stars above. Falling back into the long grass, the wind would hymn in his ears and strum the tune of his heart and he’d feel the Earth rotate beneath his fingertips. Those were the only times he felt freed. Otherworldly.

On his walk back to the stone mill he passed a young woman carrying a child in her arms.

"Que age a l'enfant?" he asked.

"Six mois," she replied wile gazing down at the child.

He dreamt that night of the woman. He'd waded the coastline to a cave where the water crashed and swirled. Inside he descended carved steps of stone laid by none know they. Wails emanated as he quickened his steps - descending further downwards and towards the flickering glow of phosphorescence that reflected off the rocks and enflamed his eye.

Entering a wide cavern, robed figures spoke in questioning tones over the balled babe and hunched figure of the woman he had passed earlier that day. As he got closer the cries of the child faded and the foremost of the cloaked figures spoke to him - asking him to pay his respects to the Gods. The man knelt before the band of cloaks and laid his palms to the ground in an act of penitence. After a moment he sensed the woman rising before him and raised his head from the ground in response. A large beak and hawkish eyes protruded from the white satin dress the woman wore. Blood dripped from her beak and clawed hands and the man realized the babe laid dead at the feet of the hawkish being before him.

"Why?" he asked.

"One asks questions for that which cannot be understood," replied the woman. "It is impossible to convey that which does not rest upon the geometry of your world. If the babe had not awoken one night and instead been found dead by your people in the early hours of the morning would you ask the same question? Would you turn to malevolent forces or the grace of a higher power to bring forth meaning? Why must every event have some causal association? The cosmos does not spin upon the crux of predetermined ambitions. Happenstance and the foundations of human life can only be perpetuated on chaos. Upon the indeterminate and unbiased series of neither fortunate nor unfortunate events. How could it be rationalized that a benevolent power laid stake in a world so commonly apt to atrocity? You believe what you want to believe."

And then he woke up.

name: after_dark.md
text:
the tug of the Coriolis
from the turnings of the world
the calling of whippoorwills
from a darkened treeline
and a full moon
from above
like a flashlight

name: merchant_ship.md
text:
Lo, the restless mountain stream.

Blue jeans rolled to the ankle,
trout rising in the dappled evening light.

And above,
moving slowly across the evening sky,
a lone ship.

Being carried on it’s way,
by taut moonsails.

name: purple_pettled_peonies.md
text:
Purple pettled peonies

All bundled up

In my hands

And I got here early

Sat down and scoped the clouds

“Hey you”

name: your_morning_halo.md
text:
Big bright clouds. Castle gradient. The swirling of stars in the blackened vault.
Holes in the sky.
Holes in the sky.
And your morning halo.

name: 04_23_24.md
text:
## 04/23/24

Yesterday felt like a dream. Like I was watching myself make decisions from a place removed. Disassociated from the thoughts in my head. And it wasn't necessarily a bad day, it was simply repetitive. Sometimes it feels like I'm simply killing time. Not living it. Not enjoying it. Not making the most of it.

But this morning I woke up after a good nights' sleep and the sun is shining through the open windows which I had left open overnight. So I washed my face and brush my teeth and wrap myself in a warm blanket and sit down to write. The words didn't start easy, but they eventually came. Like a torrent. Almost as if I missed sitting down to write in the morning. As if the words had been gathering in a jumble in my head and spiking my anxiety. Waiting for me to take the time each morning to sit down and put them down on paper. 

Things I want to do this year:

1. Write more
2. Be more social
3. Have more fun

I'm 26. Either you get busy livin', or ya get busy dyin.

name: European Discovery.md
text:
## European Discovery

## Dec 23
Yesterday, Peyton and I’s red eye flight landed in London at 7am. Jet lagged and caffeine induced insomnia followed as I showed him around Westminster and Kensington. Later that day we check into the hotel, take a power nap, and grab dinner at Five Guys. Shortly thereafter, we got a 4 rack of Bud heavies (the king of beers) and head back to the hotel to chill in the room and listen to 2000s country. Time for liftoff 🚀 

## Dec 24
We met the group downstairs in the cafe of the hotel and boarded the coach shortly thereafter. Took the ferry across the English Channel from Dover where the waters were rough and the sky sharp granite. We got into Amsterdam later that day and checked into the hotel. Got dinner at a small restaurant where Peyton and I sat next to Joel and Lizzy who told us all about Australia. Then took a canal cruise where the young men commandeering the barge let us play music and provided everyone with unlimited white wine and Heineken. 

On the way home, we were given the option to check out the Red Light District where a few of us went into an erotic museum. The woman behind the counter kept making eyes at me which was a bit unnerving. Peyton wanted me to sit in this chair that had steel neck and wrist straps. It will look funny he said. 

We finished with a quick loop where several individuals tried to sell us coke and we passed by some naked women dancing in rented red rooms/windows — one of which made eye contact and tried to lure me inside. 

Topping off the night at the bar, we did shots and danced on stage with the DJ. I talked to Daphne about neuroscience and literature. Took an Uber home with her, Tania, and Peyton. Discussed relationships and getting older. It was a good day. Onwards and upwards.

## Dec 25
It’s Christmas morning and we head over to “Winter Paradise” here in Amsterdam. Ice Skating, tubing, hot chocolate and artificial snow. Around lunch we get back on the coach and head south to Sankt Goar which is a small town that resides on a river in the Rhine Valley. Dinner is a traditional German bratwurst with a few other potato based dishes. Afterwards, we do wine tasting with a local grower and then do Secret Santa as a group. The wine is sweet and the beer smooth here in the heart of Bavaria. Looking forward to our night out in Munich. 

## Dec 26
We rise and have breakfast as a group. After which we go out to the Rhine and stand in the early dawn light and watch the river pass us by. There are little homes and big castles in the hills on either side adjoining the river. Sankt Goar is quiet and idyllic on the day after Christmas. 

We spent most of the day on the coach commuting to Munich, but reach the Bavarian capital as the day’s light fades and check into the hotel. We meet our tour guide in the city’s center and stroll the streets and take in the steeping gothic-like churches that are scattered across the city. At one point we cross through the city’s center which has a large public park and there are no lanterns lit and you could hardly see the graveled path beneath your feet.
