We finish the walking tour at a large beer hall and tip the guide. Inside, there are large wooden tables and steins of beer sit empty in front of red faces. We ordered pork knuckle and half a chicken and the man next to our table sits passed out with face down. A beautiful beer maiden glides to and fro and offers us pretzels but I can’t find the words to ask her my way. 

We board the coach and call it an early night. Listen to Yellow by Coldplay and doze as the village of Munich passes by. 

## Dec 27
We begin the day by heading to Dachau concentration camp outside of Munich. Walking the graveled footpaths into camp — treading the footfalls of so many that came and never went — we enter through corrugated steel gates and wander through a series of SS barracks and the sleeping quarters of prisoners alike. It’s a solemn morning. The grounds are quiet and a man-made creek that lines the outskirts of the camp gurgles by. Eventually I break off from the group and make my way solo through the crematorium. That’s when it really hits and it’s a bit overwhelming to be on a vacation traveling Europe and standing in a place like Dachau. I feel privileged beyond measure. 

We leave Munich headed south for Austria. It’s a long drive but the Alps begin to tower on either side and the morning flies by. We arrive in Innsbruck and spend the afternoon wandering cobblestone streets with the other tourists. Later in the day we take a gondola up into the mountains and spend golden hour at 6250 ft. 

After checking into the hotel we spend a night enjoying a traditional Tyrone evening. I’m surprised at the ingenuity and dedication to craft that the family performing illustrates. I also realize halfway through the show that these people look strikingly familiar to my Mom’s side of the family whom I know are of German descent. Contemplations of my family roots ensue.

We get back to the accommodation and the hotel bar is closed so we get beers from the vending machine (of all places) and Tiera, Arla, Peyton, and I go back to our room and we spend a few hours talking life. Another good day. Need to get some sleep though. 

## Dec 28
Today it’s Venice. Cobbled streets and gondolas and canals. Wine and Aperol Spritz. We do a walking tour with a native Venetian and she tells us how the city’s population grows smaller and smaller with each passing year. It’s a shame a city as beautiful as Venice is so impractical to live in. Many beautiful things are like this. Impractical that is. 

After the walking tour finishes we have a couple hours and a group of us find a restaurant to sit down at. It’s a rather fancy place with red and white checkered tablecloths and plated metal silverware, but we just ask for a drink menu. Peyton and I order a bottle of Sauvignon Blanc and the rest of the group drink Aperol Spritz. We get wine drunk and talk amongst new friends and wander the narrow streets, eventually making our way back to the waterfront. 

The remainder of the group joins up and we enjoy dinner together. The pasta is superb and the house wine nurses the softening buzz from the aforementioned Sauvignon. 

We get into the hotel rather early and head across the street to the bar. A dozen or so of us on the tour are there and we play Uno and Jenga and drink Captain Coke made by a bartender who has never heard of such a thing. The Aussies show us a game called Cheers to the Governor and a newly found comradery takes form. 

## Dec 29
The morning is spent aboard the coach once again as we make our way towards Rome. We check into the hotel, wash up, and head to the city center where we take to the Spanish steps and wander beneath warm Christmas lights. The city is busy, many of which are tourists, but the towering Roman pillars and Egyptian obelisks serve as a consistent reminder as to where we are, those that came before, and what shall come after when we return to dust. 

## Dec 30 
The group left early for the Vatican and I slept in. I’ve seen God’s glory from those halls two times before and some recuperation and quiet after 8 days of social travel is much needed. After getting cleaned up and grabbing something to eat, I take the early afternoon underground train down to the San Saba neighborhood and through the gates of the Protestant cemetery that resides there. The gravestones and pyramid evoke the memory of a previous visit to this same place in years past. I’m a bit surprised by the number of who have made this same pilgrimage to the resting place of Keats and at the same time I’m not. 

There are two wooden benches and I take a seat. I pull up some of his poems on my phone and am reminded why his words resonated so much to a younger self. Truth is beauty, beauty truth. Much of life’s intricacies could be unwrapped in that statement alone. 

After returning to the hotel, Peyton and I grab some beers from the nearby grocery store and step outside to the seating area beside the hotel. We’ve invited Tiera and Arla and the conversation is both easy and engaging. Eventually more of the group walk by outside and join for beers — among them Jayden, Ben, and Kyle. We call it an early night in preparation for a big NYE ahead. 

## Dec 31 
We rise early and make our way north to Florence. Starting with a walking tour through the cobbled streets we admire Michelangelo’s marble statue of David — with his veiny forearms and larger than life hands. I can’t help but admire the craftsmanship and years of effort. A true artist. In the same square, the bronze statue of Perseus standing over the body of Medusa while holding her head aloft. Greek mythology in an Italian piazza. Checks out. 

Arriving at the hotel, we get cleaned up and make our way to the city’s center along the river for a night out to celebrate New Year’s Eve. The guys make half-ass attempts to dress up and the girls are dressed to the nines. The bar has karaoke and Peyton and I get dragged onstage by Tania and Kate to sing Die Young by Kesha. Thank god we’re a dozen drinks deep. 

We leave the bar at 3am and struggle to find a taxi given the tram is down for the night. Sleep finally comes way past 5am with a 7am morning call ahead. Onwards and upwards. 

## Jan 1
It’s another big coach day as we head from Florence to Lucerne and everyone is incredibly tired. I doze off for a few hours and awake to the steeped slopes of the Swiss Alps and their snow covered caps.

## Jan 4
Last day as a group. 

Closing thoughts? Final feelings? 

Europe. A return to form. Traditions that have been long contemplated and even longer enacted. The change of pace is refreshing. Stop trying to move so fast and seek ease in the simplicities of life. Cafes, wine, and midnight walks along the river. Affection originated here. Love too. Probably beneath the Eiffel Tower with a bottle of wine in hand (if I had to guess). 

How can one stumble the cobbled streets of Florence or Paris and not entertain romantic notions?

name: Running Journal.md
text:
## Running Journal

**IT'S FRIDAY AFTERNOON** and lots of people are having friends over or going to someplace nice for dinner. That's what people do I guess. So I text a few friends and see what their doing tonight and if they want to grab drinks after work or something, but no one responds till later in the evening when its clearly too late to hang out so I just sit in my apartment anxious for a couple hours and wonder if my friends even like me or if I'm simply "tolerated". Eventually my anxiety peaks and I cave and drive to the dispensary and buy some edibles and sit in my car in the parking lot of the dispensary on a Friday night; alone and scared for no reason other than that its a Friday and I have nothing to do. _When was the last time you did something for the first time._ So I pop a gummie and take my 4Runner to the Interstate and drive out of the city and into the rural country which exists beyond every urban center and stop on dirt roads in the middle of nowhere and think I see werewolves hovering amongst the shrouded dark of the nearby treelines but it's only the Kush. A bad trip is all. I get back in my car and turn up the radio, "_Rain, rain, rain, rain...I catch a groove like, uh-uh..._" but I'm pretty scared and wonder what exists in the places beyond the reach of sunlight. 

I make it back to the truck and hop onto the hood where I lay and stare at the night sky. Think about Tiera and that night in Paris months ago where it felt so real. Wonder where she is. Reminds me of that John Mayer song, "_I don't think I'm gonna go to LA anymore_", and I sing along softly cause there's no one around to hear it, "Not sure that I really ever coulllldddd".

***

**I REMEMBER** living in London. I was 21 years old and had fancy notions of the world and where my place was gonna be in the future of it. And in those 5 months I did nothing more than drink cheap Americanos, scribble lines from romantic poets on the inside of a notebook, and get drunk by myself in my dorm room. I'd get a 6 pack of Strongbow Dark Fruit and hang them out the window where the cool winter air would keep them cool for me. Then I'd get good and drunk and my lonliness wouldn't feel so all encompassing and I'd actually be happy and the words on the page so much more fanciful and the world a bit brighter. Than I'd pass out, wake up hungover, regret last night's decisions and plan for another day alone in the city. And for all the negative sentiment in that simple memory, I just look back and think how much I have grown as a person, but what have I learned since then? I mean really? Sure, I can handle my liquor a bit better and have a close group of friends I can lean on, but what of the emptiness in my head that creaps up when I stare out the window for too long? Maybe that's why a romantic partner sounds so appealing. To quell the nothiness that wants to buzz in my head like rampant TV static. 

---

**COLLIN LEFT TODAY**. I woke up at 5am and he was still watching YouTube in the living room. Slept all day and got up and left. It was good to see him. I hope he gets well. 

After work I went into town and picked up groceries. It took me ages to find the English Muffins, which was all for naught. Burned the bacon and overcooked the eggs. Breakfast for dinner a failed attempt. But I lay here on the couch and am reminded I am well. I’ve been better, but I’m still good. Why waste in existential anxiousness when today’s newest Netflix rom-com makes me smile. 

I still think of Tiera. Our storied past still haunts me. 

There were grey rain clouds broken in parts and blasted across the sky this evening. Will they reform? There’s so much open space between the mountains and the clouds. We used to fly blimps, airships, and dirigibles. Rise up and float.

---

**TODAY WAS WEIRD**. It was cloudy for the first time since being here in Flagstaff this winter. I kept looking out the window thinking someone was about to pull in. And I wouldn’t say I’m anxious, it’s feels more like I’m restless. 

So I went to the gym, even though it was supposed to be a rest day, and did cardio at a packed Planet Fitness. It reminded me of the YMCA when I was first getting my start. That inclusive environment. Makes me wonder how many people are there for their first time and how many are destined for a lifetime of wellness. 

Picked up a Dominoes pizza afterwards (I know, hypocritical given what I just said about physical fitness). But it somehow felt warranted. Sometimes I need a pizza and a podcast and a slow night alone on my bed typing away in my notes. Boredom breeds creativity. 

Anyways, see you round. Don’t forget me. I loved you in my dreams. 

---

**YOU'RE ATTRACTED TO** emotionally manipulative women

Because I’m emotionally vulnerable?

I like helping people stabilize their emotions

At great expense 

I’ve always wanted to float down a river 

I’m attracted to emotionally positive women

I get dragged down easily 

You over process your emotions 

---

**THE SNOW** came over the mountains. It swirled all morning. By afternoon there was clumps of it hanging from my window. 

After work I grabbed groceries and went to see Dune Part 2. Brilliant fucking movie. Sci-Fi done right for a change. You should be intrigued and fascinated and scared all at once. 

When bringing groceries inside I caught sight of the sky and the brightness of the stars took me aback. I had to set the groceries down and look up and wonder for the first time in a while. 

When did we stop dreaming?

---

**IT WAS A REST DAY TODAY**. That means no gym, a shower, coffee, and finishing the book I've been reading on the couch in the early morning dawn. And while this doesn't have the same energy as turning on the warmup playlist and drinking some pre-workout, it's reinvigorating in a very distinct way. It's almost like meditating on the day's endless opportunities without crawling out of bed and being driven to immediate action. Maybe there is consolation in pondering what's to come while enjoying a given moment? 

After the morning ritual I drove into Flagstaff and grabbed some Panda Express for lunch. Orange chicken and steamed white rice. Need to take more advantage of that. 

In the early afternoon I somehow worked up the motivation to work on my car, installing a new skid plate and fog light overlays. Happy with the results, I FaceTime'd with Zack for a while and discussed his girl troubles before closing the garage door and driving up the dusty fire road at the end of my street and into the foothills of the mountains. I took a new path northward and found myself going 40mph along the ridgeline of the San Francisco peaks and watching the minute traffic crawl along 89-A and felt set apart. Freed from everyone and everything else that wasn't there in that moment with me. 

And now I sit here, sipping Old Forester 1897 and writing a day's experience that I can only hope will be re-read in some future time and place by someone who cares. By someone who actually wants to read this. Where is this person? Do they even exist? I'd like to think there's a beautiful woman somewhere in the not-so-distant future who would love to read this memoir. If that day ever comes I wish you well babe. Give me a hug and lets talk about where we came from, who we want to be. How we get there.

---

name: Unpublished.md
text:
## Unpublished ##

## Dec 27 — Innsbruck
We get back to the accommodation and the hotel bar is closed so we get beers from the vending machine (of all places) and Tiera, Arla, Peyton, and I go back to our room and we spend a few hours talking about dating and relationships and how best to convey feelings of attraction to the opposite sex (to which Peyton said it’s eye contact :p). Tiera followed this by asking what to do if you’re “shy” and it felt like flirting when I touch her arm. 

## Dec 28 — Venice
Saw T kissing Jayden at the bar — def crushing on her — hard to watch. 

## Dec 29 — Rome
T didn’t show any interested to Jayden today. Asked Peyton about it and he gave me his typical excuse, “Don’t ask me bro, I don’t understand women”. He is useless.

## Dec 31 — Florence 
Tiera, Daphne, and I leave the bar at 3am. We struggle to find a ride and I’m doing my best to navigate, but can’t help but think about T and the bar and the first 15 minutes of this new year with her. 

We eventually find a cab and make our way back to the hotel and T comes back to my room. She uses the bathroom and I sit down on the edge of the bed, taking off my shoes. She comes out and we make small talk a moment and I think she’s gonna leave, but she doesn’t; she stays. 

And I don’t know the first thing about describing sex — but it felt like time slowed down. 

*Sitting on the edge of the bed. Sliding down the strap of her bra and kissing her shoulder. Stopping for a moment and just have our foreheads pressed together. Rubbing noses.* 

Idk, how can you put these kinda things into words?

*Her on top. My hands. Finishing.* 

Does that make sense?

*Watching her stand. Pulling on her dress. Making eye contact.* 

How bout now?

*Her in the doorway. Saying goodbye.* 

And me here, a thousand miles away, still thinking about her.

name: Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 16-00.md
text:
# Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 16:00

Long day at work today. Exceptionally long, once again. I was visibly
frustrated with Miles, Amanda, Finn, and company.

I publicly updated my Slack status so folks would know I was busy.
There's a part of me that regrets doing that. That I was just supposed
to eat shit and get it done. But that feels disingenuous---to both me
and to Amanda. It starts to feel like political positioning.

There's something here about having devs respect my engineering quality
again. I haven't had to think about that in a while.

Having Finn around helps.

When I vent, it's like Amanda can tell.

People look at me like: this guy is smart as fuck. And it's not just in
one lane. I can go deep on backend development or jump into calls with
customers and problem-solve in real time.

name: Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 22-21.md
text:
# Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 22-21

THC makes me aware of my tension, and sometimes it's too much, whereas
other times I can work my way out of it---a stretch and mindfulness
combo. Alcohol makes me forget about it, neglect myself.

What if I'm running away from all my insecurities, avoiding to the point
where I have no confidence? I think you grew past that years ago,
though. That is one data point.

The idea of working memory---I overthink things because I worry I'm
going to forget them, which is mostly true. I need to be feeding my
present memory into working, even icebox, versioning.

Claude should know every which way I'm thinking so it can help me manage
me---aka you. Keep track of my email, to-do, quotes, journal. Use that
markdown editor from back in your writing days. Enter your journals.
Daily AI.

I feel like I've been confrontational lately---pushing my ideas in
conversation because deep down I know I'm right, but it's still hard for
me. Feels like I shouldn't be doing it.

My energy has been more aggressive because I've been stressed---pushing
for what I need, which feels wrong, but sounds right when I jot it down
like this. That's why I was so aggressive on Hinge, although I hated
that in hindsight.

Feels like it's been a long time since I've caught a wave. I almost had
it a couple weeks ago, but got caught up at work and lost it.

At the same time, I've really been enjoying work---almost too much---and
not taking care of myself.

name: Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 22-24.md
text:
# Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 22-24

Currently in NYC, sitting at the trail where people run on blacktop,
wandering around with a film camera, shouting a lot at buildings, jagged
skylines.

I'm the guy wandering around, looking curious, seeing beautiful people
together. Asked a girl for a shot---her and her friend in gothic
outfits. She was flattered.

Smoked a pre-roll. Meditated on a park bench. Wandered around the Upper
East Side. Found a tunnel. Shot some photography. Rogue vlogger. Journal
vlog. Travel vlog. Rogue journalist. Mayhem.

More life. Present moment. Still high, made my way to a cocktail lounge.
Weird-ass disco music, but they make a good old fashioned. Living on the
edge of things. Don't need much---just freedom from expectations.

Going west to find peace, east for inspiration. New York---center of
things. Supposed to live crammed in together. Doing it for the
experience, the growth.

I was most attracted to Kate in Europe, yet she kept going for Peyton.
Maggie saw that, told Kate---that's why Kate came back to me. I felt
like a failure in that respect, though she saw my deal.

Upper East Side this morning---higher class. Bankers and their
girlfriends. Exclusive fitness clubs. Running clubs.

Upper West Side this afternoon---golden hour in a quaint black shop.
Chatted with an Englishman working behind the counter. Got hit on by a
gay guy with a book about Greek homosexuality under his arm. "I'm
Greek," he says with a blush.

Walked four blocks to an Italian restaurant. Seated upstairs overlooking
the kitchen.

Back in the hotel after lunch. Rode the bus with regular New
Yorkers---immigrants in flannels and east side brownstones.

Four sausage pasta with ricotta, broccoli, and chive. Bread with olive
oil. Pinot grigio and sparkling water.

Need a walk in Central Park. Find a public restroom. Sit on a park
bench. People watch. Smoke.

Flagstaff. So many realizations. I've been too locked in every moment.
Haven't been relaxing---just pushing. Not sitting back and letting the
moment come to me.

Can hear the dogs and the coyotes call at night when stepping out for
air, seeing the sky.

Eating better helps me feel better.

name: Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 22-28.md
text:
# Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 22-28

Clean-shaven for the first time in five years. Truly clean-shaven---down
to a razor shave. I forgot what I looked like. The definition of my
face.

You could tell how much my skin appreciated a wash and moisturize.

Realization that I've been obsessive. I want to drop things. Just
believe everyone wants the best for you. Open those kind eyes.

Ideas. Drive the 4Runner up into the mountains. An AI project. Synthetic
data---you need synthetic data for everything. Life changes.

I want to be more social. Do fun things with other people.

How do I find my community? The other weird people. Being around other
people.

name: Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 22-45.md
text:
# Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 22-45

What do I truly want to do?

Go to someplace off the beaten path---South Africa, South America. Keep
traveling. Shoot photography. Get high down by the river. Go exploring
on foot.

More friends I actually like hanging out with in St. Paul.

I'm having good ideas, and I like where my brain is at. But there's a
voice in my head that keeps stopping me with: "oh yeah right...that's
too much."

name: Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 23-19.md
text:
# Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 23-19

Being yourself---both the simplest yet hardest thing to do.

Ordered elk because I want meat, and because I forgot about elk in the
microwave and felt bad to Jim and Marsha for wasting a gift. But the
intent was genuine---so is there truly any fault?

Spent four months trying to figure out what's wrong, and instead I've
been not being myself---scared, minimized, disengaged. Neglecting who I
was---not forgotten.

It feels foreign to sit and write---not to accomplish anything, but just
to write. I forgot what it was like to smile and publish, to not care
who sees. I have the presence of a scared rabbit---or bear---or both.

Working in a restaurant---simplified processes, different expectations
for different roles. What is it like to work in a role that doesn't
require complex reasoning?

A girl taking photos of food avoided my eye contact---probably more shy
than I am.

Quit lifting a week ago. Stretching every day. Releasing every day.
Feeling better every day.

I come off as extremely personable---to the point where in meetings with
prospective customers, I'm the most grounded person on the call.

The trial---Socrates debating piety and what is noble to the gods---and
we see him embody it in spirit.

At my root, I think I'm extremely avoidant. Conflicted so much with my
dad that I need to start saying yes to most things.

No, Aidan---you give yourself too tall of an ask sometimes.

I am the worst version of myself when I'm coming out of a
relationship---hyper-indulgent, avoidant. I slip into it and I know it's
coming.

Normal people don't have these levels of thought. I'm a poet---have that
caliber, that shake of jib.

Thinking ahead to a woman having to live with me, deal with my shit. The
only way out is through. Substack. Picture leads to picture. Hidden
door.

I'm avoidant when I'm not feeling well---deep anxiety or
depression---spirals.

It's not Buddhism or emptying your mind. It's using your brain, your
charisma. Lean into it, not away from it.

Woke up exhausted. Didn't want to get out of bed. Muscles feel tight.
Had a couple glasses of wine last night---doesn't feel like that's the
cause.

Hiked to Berthing cave. Two-plus hours in the car. Two hard paths the
last couple days. I don't know.

Oh---that's the one I was missing. Everything has two parts.
Spirituality.

I have long-term memory loss---or rather, I'm constantly filling it up.

You don't realize how much energy you burn sometimes.

Everything is a rebounding of things---sympathetic, spiritual.

When you feel overwhelmed---when your brain feels hyperactive---it's
because you forgot to look after yourself.

By being so present, I forget my needs.

I need someone who could help look after me---or rather, that's where I
was at. Where I am now, I don't need it.

Last night scrolling reels---saw a girl talk about men: unattractive and
low confidence, then medium fit with insane confidence, then very fit
but can't make eye contact.

I feel like I fall into that last category.

name: Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 23-27.md
text:
# Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 23-27

There have been periods in my life where I've had charisma---that air of
confidence---and there have been times where it felt rude and arrogant.
I only recognized that in hindsight, and there's something in that
realization that scares me. It makes me think I'm acting out of ego more
often than I realize.

But I think anyone who knows me knows I'm not like that. They try to
encourage me to come out of my shell---to just act naturally, that old
song.

When I don't feel 100%, I shell up and won't engage. At least that's how
my parents used to see it. As I get older and more mature and true to
who I am, I think my parents are starting to realize that it's isolating
being so smart---hard to engage when you process on a level abstracted
from most people.

Mantras: manage yourself first, then others.

I don't know what I want. I have a semblance of what I need. But what I
do know is that I don't want to think about it anymore---I've got to go
live it.

People who seem to understand my energy better than anyone else, parents
excluded: Hunter (though he can't quite meet me there), Alex
(sometimes), Kean, Zane.

Not thinking about yourself. All the stray cats---trying to tame them
just for me.

Work---having AI review our PRs. Suspect David will be supportive, but
he's been out of office.

name: Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 23-28.md
text:
# Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 23-28

Work---BEAD gap analysis for Mississippi, NIPA pre-screening for
Minnesota, all the config work being done by Finn and Sandesh.

I appreciate having someone else on the team pitching and supporting
their ideas. Having Anneta grill them with semi-IRLFing questions.

Organizing that call with Olga to facilitate, and purposefully being
checked out. I can't be the only one taking action items all the time.
Lead by example---report it onto others.

The dynamics of Bree, Keely, and Jen.

Jen isn't just an emotional black hole---akin to Grand back in the day.
I have experience managing her personality type, but sometimes it feels
impossible.

Bree is a different beast. Her and Michelle---mean girls---not afraid to
strut and ruffle feathers. They use that to intimidate men. That doesn't
work on me. I don't get intimidated---or if I do, not by others,
especially men. That's not even a thought that enters my mind.

Girls like Bree and Michelle lean on that dynamic. It either intimidates
men (who they lose respect for), or it doesn't intimidate them and makes
them attracted. But many men are turned off by it. Paradoxical.

I didn't say this to Keely, but I wanted to: Bree is treating me as
competition.

Jen is semi-oblivious, caught up in her own world. I think Keely knows
that on some level but won't admit it.

Frankly, I don't give a shit. I'm just trying to do my job---which seems
to attract them even more.

They look at me as bearing a lot of emotional weight right
now---something most men can't do. Dustin can, but he's inherently
emasculated.

Few men can sit in a masculinity with purpose.

name: Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 23-30.md
text:
# Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 23-30

I know the other women at Ready see those pictures from
Charleston---both envious and curious, intrigued.

I'm good at pretty much everything. More technical---or at least able to
discourse with our engineering leads. I can go to customers, fix and
improve things everywhere, foster discussion across teams and domains.

And on top of all that, I shoot film photography and have very close
relationships with our leaders---Keeley, Ewan, Amanda, Yanetta, Logan.

People like Mike respect me and are very impressed. Jace too, although
he tries not to play favorites.

Slept on my back last night for the first time in a long time, and I
woke up feeling so good. Days are starting to stack on one another.

Sam Harris this morning---meditation and dualism, that there's more than
one form of consciousness. Consciousness is manifold, multiple states of
being.

Last night and this morning, just in a pissy mood. It's not lack of
sleep or dehydration as far as I know---just annoyed with everyone.

My friends---for soaking up my attention and never returning it on par.

Engineering leadership at Ready---for being so cautious, too scared to
rock the boat.

I need a challenge. But per Hegel, I need a societal witness so I can
rise up those ideas and confirm their brilliance.

name: Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 23-31.md
text:
# Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 23-31

I just remembered how Peyton and Jack treated me in high school---or
rather how I was at the lowest point of my life. I was contemplating
suicide, my parents screaming at each other every day when I got home,
and they just cut me off.

They stopped inviting me to things, made fun of me, purposely wouldn't
tell me about things, told others to avoid me because I was being salty.

It felt like the world was pulled out from under me---the most unhappy
I've ever been.

Yet I made amends. I kept in touch. I'm the one who constantly feels bad
when I don't respond to them.

I've been at a point in my growth where I've been subconsciously
thinking about cutting them off for years. It's rooted in not feeling
connected to them anymore.

I owe it to myself to continue on my own path.

A walking beam of light. Command attention wherever you are.

It's not about looking for something to do, nor inflecting or ruminating
on what you should have done. It's about being a positive
observer---letting the subconscious rise and fall and steer, intervening
only to actualize.

Mom texted me---her and dad coming down to Phoenix this weekend to visit
Dana and Mesa. She asked to stay over Sunday through Wednesday.

And I know deep down that it's too much---or rather that I don't want
anyone staying over right now.

I'm happy and spiritually balanced for the first time in a long time.

And having Zane move in with me has minimized the time I get to be truly
alone---to be and go through all my shit.

name: Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 23-32.md
text:
# Journal Transcription -- 2026-03-28 23-32

Seeing the Matrix again---instead of trying to think less or blotting
out stress, just do it. Be unapologetically brilliant.

You'll say that because that's what you want.

Lifting more weight than anyone else for as long as I did in my
circle---been off two months and that sense of confidence is starting to
come back.

What a good thought---to compound more often, instead of being too
strict on your ego.

I've loved girls who certainly don't love me back. Feels like that's how
it starts.

But I'm at a place now where I always fall in love first, and at least
part of me is struck---like women know they've got me.

And everyone has lost me, and they feel that.

name: Transcription -- March 28, 2026, 03-58 PM.md
text:
Well I'm in a relationship I feel like I have to some I have to have some alone time to decompress because when and Andrea we make fun of me for shit that I do but I really don't like being laughed at which you ask everyone else to take you serious I feel like I'm mature Beyond David in that way always on and Jack positive mindset and Peyton take things at least a little serious I've never dated someone secure feel like I've lost my age. The masculine tendency to disagree and tell people who are included—call them out of their shit—and everyone expects me to be nice. Deemed unfair when I'm hurt or say something that I know strikes at someone's emotional core because I have a better sense of that than anyone else I know.

Mom and Dad are empathetic, but I wouldn't say they live in the same emotional awareness that I do. I have a deeper understanding of self and others than anyone else I've ever met. [He] comes close, but he lacks internal controls to use it wisely. People don't like to admit that I'm right so often; it makes them feel insecure. Jack is a perfect example of this. Peyton, sometimes more recently, which is odd.

I've been growing, which makes me want to call them out. Since no one likes to have their own anxiety, people simply avoid them and go on like everything is fine, but that's what I'm going for. That's what I always respected about Dad: he called you out on your shit, just never his own, unfortunately. You start to realize everyone gets thrown the same sorts of problems in life. It's how you deal with them—that's what defines getting happier.

As someone who has had a lot of things already thrown at me... waking up at—woke up at 5:00 a.m. today, maybe earlier. Couldn't get back to sleep.

# Transcription -- March 28, 2026, 04:05 PM

Partially sore from a good workout. Work has been busy. Brought a lot down this week because I cut back on the devices—feeling more focused. Too many more opinionated at work, not caring if someone might take something the wrong way.

Missing home just a bit. Think if it was a long time, I truly needed it though. What do you want? Went to bed at 9:30 and woke up at 7:30. Had lots of sleep and then totally sure why I was so tired yesterday. Throat hurt really bad by the end of the day, maybe related to exercise.

Cultivated set of things you like to do. Going out for the first time to sit down in a little while; weeks. Forget how much I enjoy it. Shift Kitchen, downtown Flagstaff, for the first time. Country music.
forget how uncomfortable dying alone can be like being at the gym trying to forget the people are always watching you wish you were constantly reminded of every time you look up haven't had a good day good dry martini a long time you should get them dirty then the server asked if I wanted all of our twist and I remember that time Martini still has a garnish what are where are the things the girl next to me I could use one of those as much as I don't want to admit it a partial realization living at 7,000 feet not eating enough not drinking enough water not making the most of my network call People's letters who doesn't like getting married get something in the mail this idea come up with has come up before magazine issues

I feel an oligation to make eye contact with others here at the chef's counter—be social and fun and make someone else smile—but I want—it's been one of those weeks. Just want to sip my dry martini and journal and enjoy my food; recharge in my own way.

Remembering the present looks like it's in the presence of others. I think you spend too much time alone and forget what it is. Your brain from flights, being lost in the moment with being present in reality. Being present means acting entirely like yourself even when around large groups of people or in public. You forgot that's so easy really by yourself because they can't mimic, and humans are like imitation machines our evolution.

You forget that simply being your weird and overthinking yourself from others is a key to your presence by being social. Questioning in the fact that we are in the moment, but that was rude and her insecurities of needs to know what was going on, or rather what I was thinking 24/7, which pulls me out of my body. That's also why I'm supposedly when I'm striving for, which is why Mom made that comment. I said that you would benefit the most from him. Originally thought it was a bad thing fundamentally, but living with [him] has been difficult yet good and why I'm struggling.

name: Ad Astra.md
text:
# Ad Astra

after eons of wandering 

the murky sea

and stumbling onto land

we are here

gliding over land

flying over land

questioning and troubling over it

hoping it sustains

while we grasp at stars 

pull them down to our windows

at night

hold them

fall asleep

​       with them

and dream of the heavens 

with them

name: After Dark.md
text:
## After Dark

the tug of the Coriolis 
from the turnings of the world

the calling of whippoorwills 
from a darkened treeline 

and a full moon
           from above
like a flashlight

name: Ambition.md
text:
### Ambition

Celestial sphere. Interstellar domain. 

Engineered rockets. Ignited molecules. 

Slowly place your hands in pockets. 

And set pace, chart a course.

Put the windows down son.

Let�s enjoy the spring air.

name: Big Sky.md
text:
## Big Sky

Why is Montana called Big Sky? 

Do the mountains press up the clouds? 

Are the heavens that much farther out of reach?

I'll need an elevator to the stars.

name: For Tiera.md
text:
## For Tiera 

Tiera,

I'm sorry for saying the right things

and the wrong things

but most of all for not trying harder to make you and I work



It's the certainties of life

like the falling of rain

or death and taxes

that comes to mind when I think about you and I



And of one thing I'm certain

is that there will be 

a part of me

that regrets not taking a leap of faith

and buying a plane ticket

to at least see your smile

one last time.

name: Hang High the Roof Beams.md
text:
## Hang High the Roof Beams

I like watching the construction workers

putting up that new building.



Glitters in the sun

the early morning 

a luminous drape



And the men in orange vests

riding the cabled lines 

up in the sky.



Hang high the roof beams men.

Hang em high.

name: Let's Get Sentimental Babe.md
text:
## Let's Get Sentimental Babe

Just cause you're sad,

​	doesn't mean everyone has to be.

Let's get sentimental babe.

name: Like An American.md
text:
## Like An American

Behind skyscaper glass and digitized screens sit some of America's brightest.

They leave work and drive home to empty houses, empty fridges, empty hearts.

Spending the night scrolling through other people's lives, making plans they won't ever keep, and sending texts they already regret.

But they'll fall asleep with their window open.

Gazing at the shaking trees and above to the sattelites that look so much like stars.

And wake in the morning, eager for a new day, a new promise.

And forget they ever felt so sad.

Like an American.

-------------

Behind skyscaper glass and digitized screens

home to empty houses, empty fridges, empty hearts

scrolling through other people's lives, making plans they won't ever keep

asleep with their window open

trees above and sattelites that look so much like stars

wake in the morning

And forget they ever felt so sad

Like an American

name: Maple Leaves.md
text:
## Maple Leaves

Its the last weekend 

​	in October

and the accumulation of maple leaves

​	makes it impossible

to find your sidewalk

name: Move Cross Country.md
text:
## Move Cross Country

We used to get on a train and chase.

Move cross country.



Opportunity. Responsibility. 

One and the same. 



There's jobs in the cities. 

Over on the coast. 



Nothin but dirt here for me. 

And that friend from long ago.



Loaded the car.

I'm goin. 



Yeah, sorry. I'm leavin. 

I loved you in my dreams.

name: One Mountain, Four Angels.md
text:
## One Mountain, Four Angles

Across the valley, on mountain steep

Rose four white angels

Heads bowed to weep



Across their shoulders 

a gleaming coffin shrugged

Tears of Nations 

on its surface hung



When near the top

they stumbled and fell

The glistening coffin

an open shell



Among the dirt

a small child lay

Our Lord and savior

his price to pay

name: Parallel Rails.md
text:
## Parallel Rails

The sky looked like a crumpled newspaper

As I listened 
     to the faint sound 
Of rail cars 


Cuh Clink 
    cuh clink
Over the parallel rails


And wondered
    what resides
In those iron trolleys 


And who is it
    eyes steady
Guiding you

name: Portal.md
text:
## Portal

An archway in the desert 

​      slingshot to the heavens

​			off-ramp to infinity

​			                 portal to the stars.

name: Purple Pettled Peonies.md
text:
Purple pettled peonies 

All bundled up 

In my hands 



And I got here early 

Sat down and scoped the clouds 



“Hey you”

name: Rainbow Trout.md
text:
## Rainbow Trout

Caught a couple rainbow trout today. Threw em both back. Ten inches only. 

Beautiful fish. They were soft and dimpled in hand. Eyes raising questions. 

I had no answers.

name: The Fair.md
text:
## The Fair


Under the spinning wheel,

and neon ambience



Your feet,

aground the confetti'd footpath



Do you remember what it was like,

to wonder?

name: The Flood.md
text:
## The Flood

There was a time,

before we lived the lives of others,

that stars could be found in abundance

​                  without limit.

But then the floods came,

and life along with it.

name: The Merchant Ship.md
text:
## The Merchant Ship

Tilt your head back and take in the gaping cosmos,

​	sky a blackened vacuum.

The aggregation of blinking stars, 

​	pitching the sails of a grand argosy, 

​		plowing through the eternal vastness.

name: These Stones.md
text:
## These Stones 

When were these stones,

​	laid parallel the Earth? 

Corner to groove. 

Palm to surface. 

Heel to finished product.

name: Tumbling Astronauts.md
text:
Tumbling astronauts 

Precipate our blue origin

Steel yards 
      and silos

Maintain it 

If only to hold it in our hands 

Yet never look down

Close that aperture a moment 

And promise you won’t let go

name: Your Morning Halo.md
text:
Big bright clouds. Castle gradient. The swirling of stars in the blackened vault. 

Holes in the sky. 

Holes in the sky. 

And your morning halo.

name: 1.md
text:
## Chapter One

**Dexter**: that's me, just a regular guy with a coke problem

**Skip**: bartender at Ziggy's

**Hildie**: goes with Flattop but no one really knows why

**Ashtyn**: 

**Lex**: Ashtyn's roommate

**Flattop**: friend and coke dealer 

**Camila**: your girlfriend (if you wanna call it that)

---

**I COULD TELL YOU** how I came to be here, but that would take too long and you probably don't give a shit either. So instead, lets just stick to the facts. 

Fact #1: I've got a coke problem. Yeah, I'll admit it, that's the first step in AA right? Admit there's a problem? 

Fact #2: I don't have a job, and honestly? That's fine by me. Everyone else seems to think its this big deal, but frankly, I'm doing just fine. 

Fact #3: When I'm not high or drunk or balls deep ... I'm depressed as fuck. But that's most people I'd assume. So lets get back to real life, shall we? 

We go out most nights to Ziggy's. Its this pizza joint over on 21st and its got great pies, I mean, the best. It has something to do with how they make the dough, but I'm rambling. In regards to the other commodities, Ziggy's has a full bar (an obvious plus) and private restrooms (which make hoovering Bolivian marching powder all that much easier) and the bartender Skip is a friend of ours (he looks the other way when we get to hoovering and lets us stay past closing). So yeah, you could say Ziggy's is the place to be most nights. 

And here we are, a Tuesday night, the gang and I lining the bar with open tabs. Its pretty late, past midnight if I had to guess, but its earlybird hours for this lot. 

"Pass them wings," Hildie says to me. "I paid for those and I should get a few of 'em at least.'"

Hildie is a friend of a friend. She goes with Flattop but no one really knows why. She's gotta be at least an 8 when she's cleaned up and not 48 hours and an eight ball in, but even now, I'd still take her for a roll. And then there's Flattop. Bit of a jigsaw if you ask me. He's pretty quiet, keeps to himself, but says the craziest shit from time to time that makes me think he's a bit fucked in the head. Hildie also says he has quite the hog so that likely makes up for the lack of personality. And he's a decent looking chap, so we'll chalk their pre-marital affair up to compatible pheromones. I admire Hildie's ass as she reaches over the counter and past me to where the wings are. She's wearing those high waisted white-washed jeans with the bottoms cut short like all the girls nowadays. Likely because it hides the waistline and accentuates the hips a bit more if you ask me.

The gangs all here. Hildie and Flattop and Lex and her roommate Ashtyn. 

"Anyone needa bump?" Flattop asks the group from down the bar. I tap the bar and grin at him. We're about due for a quick hoov. 

We assemble in ones and twos, making our way to the bathroom -- we use the men's naturally -- its less conspicuous doing blow from the men's restroom. We form a line of sorts. Flattop goes into the handicap stall with Hildie and they close the door behind them. I'm standing with Lex and Ashtyn by the urinals. I try and think of something to say (since they're both out of my league) but instead opt to stare at the mirror above the sink in front of us where someone has scrawled TAKE YOUR TOP OFF in what looks to be orange crayon. I try to think back and feel the anxiety inside me rise when I can't remember if I was the one who wrote that. Does Bolivian marching powder cause memory loss? 

"Next!" Flattop calls from the stall as he bangs open the door. Hildie exits, rubbing her nose, her eyes wide and bright. I wave Ashtyn on ahead of me. "You next hun," She gives me a weird look and proceeds ahead. Hildie has stopped to look at herself in the mirror. She turns her face from side to side and looks up to make sure there isn't any coke showing from her nasal cavity. She runs her hands down her sides and flattens the blouse she's wearing and hikes up her extremely well-fitting jeans, turning halfway around and admiring her hips from the side while doing so. She approaches Lex and I, who are still standing in line for our second helping of hoov. I try and make eye contact to understand what she's doing but she glides right past me and gives Lex a big hug.

"I love you, you know that right?" she says to Lex while their embraced in a tightly held hug. "Don't let all them get to you,"

I have no idea what she's referring to at this point and I'm also trying to avoid looking like I'm watching (even though I most certainly am). They step back from their hug a bit and Hildie looks Lex in the eyes before kissing her. At this point I look up at the ceiling in idle contemplation. 

I look towards Lex and she looks nonplussed. Is this a common occurrence, I wonder? Further investigation required. Starting an OnlyFans and having them dance on camera flutters through my mind. They’d start on-camera and finish off-camera. Hehe. 

Hildie turns toward me and says “Go get me a beer Dexter.” I consider this a moment. 

“I haven’t scopped any hoov yet,” I say to her as calmly as possible. Images of me hovering over a king sized bed with Hildie and Lex scantily clad in lingerie lingers in my mind for a moment as I say this. 

“Fine,” she replies with feign attitude. “I’ll get one for you and me. Blue Moon’s with oranges.” She yums lightly too herself and goes back out to the bar. 

At this point Flattop and Ashtyn *have* to be done hoovering, so I’m not entirely sure what they’re still doing in the handicapped stall. I train my ears. No words. Then the sound of someone bumping into something. Feet scrape on the tiled floor and the door opens with a quick pull. Ashtyn walks out first, flattening her dress a bit while doing so. She walks out of the bathroom immediately. Flattop now stands in the doorway of the bathroom stall. 

“Well I’ll be,” he says to himself, rubbing his nose from the line of blow he just rocketed. “Needa find me one of them,” I think he’s gonna go on longer but he just shakes his head a bit and steps back into the stall. I immediately follow suite. He carves out a couple short lines on the diaper changing station with his matte black American Express Centurion card and gives me the nod. Without further ado I prostrate myself before the alter of our Columbian overlords and their wonderful product and behoove me a line. I feel the kick start in my nasal cavity and extend to the back of my skull and I stand with my back straight and bless myself with the cross of our Lord and savior. _Death to Judas oh Lord._ 

"You gonna hit that or what?" says Flattop and I bend down once more to complete the deed. I am happy. 

"Alright," I say "Let's get back out there."

"Sir yessir," Flattop replies with a salute. He pulls out his wallet and puts the Am Ex card back in once more, licking the card clean before doing so. He then zips up the Ziploc baggie that contains the cocaine and places it inside his jacket in a pocket that looks super secret. _I wonder what else he hides in there_. We make our way out of the bathroom and back to the bar where Ashtyn, Lex, and Hildie are talking to the bartender Skip. 

Flattop tries to sit in the barstool next to Hildie but she pushes him off saying "Dexter is sitting here. That's his beer right there," and she points to the Blue Moon with the orange pierced upon the rim. I step forward and shrug to Flattop as I take the seat. He goes across the bar and sits on the other side of our group over by Ashtyn and Lex. 

"Blue Moon is the best beer," Hildie seemingly says to herself but I can tell its directed at me. 

"Hell yeah it is," I reply. "And the orange provides the Vitamin C that protects the body from infectious disease. There could not be a more complimentary beverage for such a setting as this," and I take a sip from the beer Hildie had bought me and I can momentarily taste the truth of the statement.

"Shut the fuck up Dexter. No one gives a rat's ass about Vitamin D when they're coked up and sipping a drink. I was talking about the _ascetic_ pleasure of Blue Moon."

"Right on. One hundred percent yo," I say and nod my head to make it clear she's right and always has been right and I may or may not have feelings for her but then turn my head back to my beer and consider what a dumbass I must look like to her and everyone else sitting at the bar. _I need to change the subject._

"So...uhhh...yeah...I mean...what's it like kissing a girl..."

"Shut the fuck up Dexter. You would like to know that wouldn't you."

"I mean..."

"Men are all the same aren't they. When they aren't groping you over a line of blow they're entertaining fantasies of you with other women. So unoriginal Dexter. I expected better from someone like you."

"What does that even mean..." I ask.

"It means you're boring."

"Oh...okay," I was expecting something a lot worse if we're being completely honest. Like for Hildie to bring up how someone has been following her around in a Toyota 4Runner that looks suspiciously like mine and how someone broke into her apartment a few weeks ago and nothing was missing, but her lingerie was scattered across the floor and there appeared to be semen on the carpet. _Still oblivious I guess_ and I shrug to myself with a relieved smirk. 

"What are you smiling about," Hildie says.

"Uhhh...nothing...how much I love Blue Moon?" 

She smiles at this and I know I've played the situation cool. Real cool.

They try half-heartedly to get me to stay for a few hours longer, but I am resolute -- even as Hildie guilt trips me about the half drank Blue Moon still sitting on the bar. At this, I grab the glass and knock the remainder back, place it soundly back on the bar, and slide it to Skip's awaiting hand. I then turn to Hildie with a half smile and she glares back at me as I exit Ziggy's and step into the cool summer night.

The drive home is uneventful other than the multiple times I almost fall asleep and am awoken by oncoming headlights. Once home, however, I walk to my upstairs bedroom and find my old iPhone 12 and log in with the passcode my ex-girlfriend had told me one too many times. Opening the "Find my iPhone" app I look to see where she is and am relieved to see that she is at her parent's place on west side. Next, I check her recent text messages and its more of the same; her shit talking me to her closest friends. There's several texts along the lines of "I've always known Dexter wasn't right for me" and "I'm better than this" to which all her friends claim to have seen our breakup coming and respond with things like "Fuck that guy" and "You can do so much better" to which I laugh since the girl who says this is most certainly NOT a hardbody. _Women attribute so much of their self-worth to men's perception of them. It's rather shameful._

It amazes me sometimes how I'll check my phone and know there is nothing new that could possibly await -- but we do it anyway don't we? One never knows when the next dopamine spike will present itself now do we. I find a clean whiskey glass from the cupboard and mix a drink, making it on the weaker side knowing that I may have to perform later. Once mixed to ample proportions I grab the drink and step into the living room and pull up YouTube. All manners of perverse videos are shown on my homepage -- all of which try and snatch my attention -- but the drink in my hand reinforces what I came here for and I search for "hoe math", soon finding the content creator's page and seeing that he has uploaded several new "shorts" since I had last tuned in. The first one is titled "Nice Guys" and I smirk to myself wondering what this will entail. The video starts with the fifteen second clip of a woman ranting, presumably on TikTok, about "guys always saying their 'nice guys' and actually just being ugly" and I agree their is some truth in what she's saying. Its at this point that Hoe Math takes out a piece of paper and draws a stick figure with a pink skirt and golden hair which is presumably the woman in this scenario and then draws other stick figures with word bubbles sprouting from their faces that say things like "I'm one of the good ones" and "Here are some flowers" and Hoe Math goes on to explain in clear, concise, almost Calculus-like precision how this woman is simply coining the men she finds unattractive as "nice guys". These are simply the men who are genuinely decent humans -- and maybe even rather successful in their given careers -- but who lack the raw height, bulk, dominance, and in simple terms, masculinity, that she requires to have a baseline level of attraction to a man. 

My phone lights up with a "Nope" to which I respond "Come over". I start to put my phone down, but already she's responded "Sure" and I immediately jump into action, running upstairs and hopping into the my bedroom's shower. Once my body is squeaky clean I towel off and wrap it around my waist as I grab two ice cubes from the mini fridge in my room and start massaging them into the areas under my eyes which are dark and puffy from all the drinking and coke; I can only do this for a minute or two however, before my fingers and eye sockets bite from the ice burn and I throw the cubes into the bathtub to melt away. I check my phone again and no response and then take out my second phone and check her location on Find My iPhone and see that she is already halfway here and I momentarily panic, but lean into the fact that she is rushing over here to see me and remember that I have all the power in this relationship. After two deep breaths I pull on my Calvin Klein joggers and a freshly washed North Face hoodie and go downstairs. I turn the TV down and change it over from YouTube to Hulu and pull up Always Sunny in Philadelphia which is her favorite show (even though I think it lacks originality). I check my second phone again and see she is less than a minute away and sip my drink to quell what remains of my nerves and start to get aroused just thinking about her coming through the front door. I immediately drop to the floor and do a handful of pushups to pump up my pectorals for when she inevitably takes off my shirt and sit back on the couch once more and its at this point that I hear a knock on the door and stand back up. 

I open the door and Camila is standing there, smiling. "Hey cutie," she says and I step aside to let her in. Once inside, I close the door and turn to face her and she leans in for a hug so I indulge, my hand pretty far down her back, but I make a point of not touching her ass, at least not yet. We break from the hug and she walks further inside, past the kitchen and into the living room, "You need to do something different with this place." I ignore her and step into the kitchen, asking her if she wants something to drink. "Something with tequila," she responds. That's what I like to hear, I mumble to myself while grabbing a bottle of Espolon Blanco as well as blue curacao, lime juice, and simple syrup from the fridge. I combine these ingredients into a thin tall cocktail glass and contemplate making a new one since I forgot to salt the rim, but ultimately decide not to, instead stepping into the living room and handing her the drink. "Thank you," she says as I sit down next to her. She takes a sip from the drink, sets it on the coffee table in front of us (forgetting to use a coaster even though I've reminded her a dozen times now) and snuggles up into my shoulder while placing her hand on my right thigh. I lift up the remote and start Always Sunny and we sit like this for a while, just enjoying each others company. Then the advertisements interject and our too loud and annoying for me to sit still through them so I ask "How was your day?" which was coincidentally was the right thing to ask at this given point in time. "I don't want to talk about work, my parents, or friends," she replies "I just came over here for you." I raise my eyes to hers and we both stand up and she takes my hand, leading me upstairs to my room. 

Once inside I raise my hand to flip the lights on, but she stops me, leaning in close and putting her arms around my torso as she kisses me. It's light and romantic for a moment before I push her down onto the bed and take my shirt off, we both know why she's here. The sex is much of the same; a variety of positions with some rougher elements mixed in -- notably the choking and gagging which she always asks for. Eventually we finish and lay there in silence, panting like animals. I reach over to my bedside stand and grab my vape and take a long rip, handing it to her when finished. She never used to smoke before we met but alas here she is. In my bed once again when we said to each other this wasn't going to happen. Hitting my vape (again) and knowing she shouldn't. 

"Good game," I say and raise my hand for a high five but Camila is not amused. Instead she throws my vape back at me and throws the covers off and stands up, pulling her dark blue thong up her tan legs and turning back towards me. "Why can't you just be serious for once," she says and bends down grabbing her dress where it lays crumbled on the ground. She throws it over her head and twists from side to side as she lets it settle back around her curves and I can't help but think she looks better with the dress on than she does with it off. Leaves the animal instinct we just indulged to the imagination. All those Baptists and Mormons doing their soaking and not having any sex must love a well fitting dress. Or leggings. Those Latter-Day Saints must love leggings. I consider saying a quick prayer to God in thanks for Lululemon and leggings and all things female that are tight fitting, but Camila has started to leave and panic swells deep inside me. 

"Ummm...I mean...you can stay and chill ya know..." I say this while standing and feel like a dumbass with my limp cock hanging in front of me like some un-watered houseplant. 

"Nope," she replies and walks down the stairs. I rush to pull my joggers back on and step quickly down the stairs, catching her at the front door as she's about to leave. 

"What's wrong," I ask and put my hand on the door, keeping her from going anywhere. 

"Take your hand off the door Dexter," she looks me right in the eye while saying this. "I don't want to see you or think of you or do this shit EVER again," and she wretches the door open from right under me. I watch her get into the brand new Ford Bronco her Dad bought her for "finishing college" and she drives off clearly in a fit of emotion given the sharp angles at which she turns out of the driveway and onto the road. Yet here I am, standing with the door open with my shoulder leaning against the frame and wondering with quiet bemusement why she keeps coming back over here. _You know why._ But I banish this thought and close the door.

name: 1.md
text:
## Chapter 1

They sat in the road on pails they grabbed from the milk house on the way out. Fireflies winked on the edge of the forest crowding the dirt road to the east. 

"There used to be men, real men that is, not these pussies nowawadays, that came over this land. Some settled, rest of em went onward. Why are we still here?" said Will.

"You need to pass that bottle over here; And no, I don' know why we're here, but I'm not looking forward to tomorrow at this rate." Teddy smirks, drinks, and looks westward. 

"We're all destined to disappear. Take this exit for nowhere," Will says quietly, seemingly to no one. "To to get away - but don't know where we're headed."

"That's some Socrates shit if I've ever heard it," Teddy responded sardonically. "Sounds like you need another drink," passing the bottle to Will. Whipoorwills sounded nearby. You had to strain to hear them. Lean forward and lend an ear. 

"Let me tell you something Teddy. You ever hear that one bout the preacher down at First Methodist? One who supposedly had them women chained up in the basement?"

"Nah - dont have no fuckin idea what you talkin bout. Sounds like a real Romeo and Juliet kinda story though. My kinda page turner."

"There was this rumor goin round for ages that he was a little too interested in the pretty half of the congregation. Wasn't married. No family in town. Been preaching nigh on six to seven years. But no one thought too much of it. No one can blame the poor bastard given him being solely in a relationship with God ya know."

"Course, makes sense," says Teddy.

"This goes on for a couple years, preacher makin a casual pass every once in a while. Then one Sunday morning, he don't show up for sermon. Few folks from the congregation go to his house across town and see his car parked out front. When the get up to the front door, they find the door open and go inside. Nothing out the ordinary on the main level - but then they go down to the basement," Will pauses and takes a drink from the bottle. "You still followin?"

"Sheet. How can someone not be following this kinda story. He's pry got kids chained up down there or some shit. Claim he's doing 'God's Work'."

"That's just it. There was no one in the basement. Only thing down there was a single spring mattress in the corner, all soiled and shit. No blanket or sheets. Just a wornout Bible sitting ontop it." Will finished slowly, eyes blank and focused elsewhere. "What do you make of that?"

"I think he was down there with them wives from the congregation. Start by reading them a bible verse and finish with some sinning - if ya know what I mean." Teddy lets out a roil of laughter and grabs the bottle from Will, taking a big swig. 

"I reckon he vanished," said Will. "Left his car, his bible, everything man. Took off into the desert on foot like some penitent."

The sun was completely down now, the last few rays catching the atmosphere and illuminating the world for the last time that day. "There is no god damn way he just took up and left. Why would he do such a thing? Don't make no logical sense," said Teddy. "People dont just take off and disappear on a whim."

"We've been reinventing ourselves for millenia. Since our beginnings we always been hunters, navigators, explorers. Seems like we lost some of that real recent. And I'm damn sure folks still walking into the mountains and never coming back. Man still looks to the west and sees freedom. Or to the stars to be called upon. Some of us just don't listen."

They sat in silence. Crickets filled the air with harmony and archaic wisdom whispered through the trees never to be uttered again. "Pass that bottle will ya."

***

**March 13, 1959**

I think this country was at it's morally finest moment during the years leading up to and following it's formation. Men used to gawk at politicians who took the stand and presented lies or half truths. They'd rather watch someone swing then fundamentally abhor their rights so proximally grounded._

_And yet here we are, a hundred years or so removed from our primordial foundations and being lied to by politicians, advertisements, the corporation that pays for your groceries and mortgage, and even our closest friends. We have become so numb to being lied to that the hope and resolve that founded this country has become tainted with equal parts cynicism and jadedness.

Who are we and where are we going?

-------------------

They road back to the old farmhouse in the '52 Chevy pickup Teddy bought from his father before he passed away. Teddy didn't like to talk about that, his father that is. As far as Will knew, Teddy and his dad were pretty close and things hadn't been good again between him and his siblings ever since. Something to do with him trying to tell them what Dad would have wanted if he were still around. 

"You planning on going out tomorrow night?" asked Will across the console to Teddy, who had his eyes locked in the darkness ahead of them. 

"Probably," replied Teddy "depends on how we're doin after stacking them small squares for old man Randolph."

"Pry won't be too bad," said Will. "Between the two of us, one in the loft and one on the rack, should be able to get a few hundred done in an hour or two. I'm more concerned with whether your gal is gonna be round downtown. Heard from Maria that she been askin bout you."

"Fuck me," Teddy softly said to himself. "I ain't given her a glance leastways and she just keeps persisting and all. What do you make of that Will? She just lookin to settle down or something? I do envy girls thatways. Know what they want and all."

"I think she sees something steady and strong. Whether being partial to you is cause of that or cause she wants kids is to be determined. I think you should buy her a drink tomorrow night, learn something instead of speculating all the time," replied Will with genuine regard. 

The truck eased right and into the driveway, dogs began to sound off. "Looks like the girls are happy to see us," said Teddy with a grin. Pulling up next to the house, he killed the engine and flipped off the headlights. Two black labs came off the front porch in bounds. "There's a good gurl," Teddy crooned. "Don't see much value in talkin up Kate when got me plenty of lovin right here at home."

Will shook his head with a soft smile. "Different kind of lovin between Kate and them dogs - I hope you know that." 

##########################################################################

_In the days before recorded time, our ancestors lived the land with bow and spear. Made fires in the hills and whispered stories with faces aglow the dying embers. At night we'd lay in deep furs on pine thicketed forest ground and stare at the starblown sky. Counting blinking stars and ascribing meaning to their shape while conversing with them in deft whisper and posing questions of the soul._

_And when the stars would fall from the heavens men would chase them. With bared feet and stooped shoulder they'd rush over the hills tracking the course of the fallen rock and stumble upon its resting place deep in the glens of the mountain. The smoldering stone gleamed with emerald and sapphire iridescence and hummed with mystery which not be of this world nor any other. Placing hand on stone they'd stare deep into the heart of the comet and ponder the spinning planets and the course whence the meteorite had come to pass and raise eyes skywards in contemplation of the cosmos._

_Align the sextant. Set a heading. Draw a footpath amongst the celestial vault._

##########################################################################

name: 10.md
text:
## Chapter 10

**THEY WERE PARKED** on the side the road. It was well past midnight and they had pulled off onto a hayfield's road approach on the way home from the bar. Teddy, Will, and Marlene sitting on the tailgate of Teddy's Chevy and Kate was standing in the middle of the road with a beer in hand. 

"Every generation has its problems," Kate said. "Our parents' had the Depression and the dole and a second world war. We've got a different problem though. We don't have problems enough. Everything is 'too easy' to our parents. 'You kids have it real good' -- how many times have I heard that? I wish our parents knew that sometimes we envy them and how simple the world used to be. Was all pretty clear when Hitler and them Nazis were killin all them Jews. Of course we gotta do somethin. Now what though? We settle down and get married and buy a house and have kids because its what our parents always wanted and some of em never got cause they got blown apart in Europe? No sir. No ma'am. It ain't that simple."

"We got no purpose," said Marlene quietly. 

"EXACTLY" said Kate as she took another drink from her beer can. "We ain't got no purpose. How do we find one of them Ted?"

"Find one of what?" said Teddy.

"A purpose gosh darnit."

"I think Ted's already got one of them," said Will with a smile.

"Whatchu say cowboy?" said Kate.

"Nothin."

"He said I got my purpose and she's right here in front of me," said Teddy and he hopped off the trailer hitch, taking the beer can from Kate's hand and setting it on the ground and pulling her close they started to dance. 

At one point Teddy lowered his hand a bit too far and Kate pushed him away abruptly,"God damnit Teddy, can't you just be sweet for a moment?!" 

Teddy shrugged at this, picked up Kate's beer can off the ground, and finished it.  "Let's call it a night gang," he said.

"Awwww, come on Ted, I was just playin with you," Kate said with tears in her eyes. 

"Nah, its late. Best be gettin back home anyways."

At this, Kate started to cry. Marlene hopped off the tailgate and went to comfort her, leading her around and helping her into the backseat of the pickup. 

"What did I do now?" said Teddy.

"Think its what you didn't do bud," said Will as he too hopped off the tailgate of the pickup. "Think Kate wanted you to keep dancin. She's just givin you a hard time is all."

"I know that."

"Then why didn't you keep dancin?"

"Cause I was havin a right fine time till she got all preen and proper with me. Why can't she just loosen up a little bit? We been datin for years now. Nothin wrong with puttin hands on each other."

"Don't think she wants to be that kinda girl, 'specially with Mar and I sitting right here watchin."

"Well shit, why does that matter?"

"I dunno. Just does."

They walked back around and hopped in the truck, Teddy behind the steering wheel as they made there way home. Kate and Marlene were looking out the window and everyone was silent. Will turned on the radio, but couldn't find a station with a signal so he turned it back off and they road in silence. Eventually they reached Kate and Marlene's new place and Teddy and Will hopped out to open the rear cab doors for the girls. Marlene was appreciative and said "Good night," but Kate shook her head and stormed inside. They closed the cab doors once more and hopped back inside and made their way back. They didn't say nothing till Teddy up alongside the farmhouse and killed the engine. "Guess I fucked up," he said. 

"Nah, don't worry bout it Ted. She'll be fine tomorrow. Just the beers and emotions and all."

"Alright," Teddy said and hung his head a bit and went into the house where the dogs were crying. Will stood on the porch a minute longer, staring at the night sky as he tried to find Orion, but there was too much cloud cover so he lowered his eyes and stepped inside. 



**WILL REMEMBERED** being a kid. Rushing out the front door and across the yard and into the fields of alfalfa that came up past his chest. He'd go out and out and further out still and sit down in the middle of nowhere and listen to the wind rustle the budding purple tips of the surrounding lucerne. Eventually his parents would come outside and call out and he could hear the concern in their voices, but he stayed hidden for a while longer. Until he'd filled his soul to the brim with the surrounding calm that can only be attained while getting lost in nature's ephemeral moments. He'd then stand, brush the dirt from his pleated corduroys, and run back to the farmhouse on the hill. He wouldn't say anything until they spotted him. "Here I am!" he'd call out reassuringly. "WHERE WERE YOU! WE WERE WORRIED SICK..." and he'd try his best to be remorse and say he'd never do it again but that was a lie. He longed for distance. To be out alone and to explore the the storied deeps. Maybe just to have some peace of mind and peace and quiet for more than the handful of minutes he found it whilst at home. 



"**WHY DON'T YOU LIKE MAR**?" said Kate.

"I do like Marlene," said Will.

"Doesn't seem that way, given you won't ask her out proper and all..."

"I know."

"Oh? Why is that?"

"I know what you're saying, but I don't really know why if that makes sense."

"That's a dumbass answer and you know it."

"I mean, I guess...sometimes I feel like...there might be something wrong with me..." Will said and threw his eyes to the skies. 

Kate looked at him quizzically a moment before saying, "There is nothin wrong with you Will".

"It's hard to explain," Will said softly this time.

"Then try to."

There was a long pause before Will replied. "I look at you and Ted and at our parents and grandparents and how happy everyone is being together and I don't feel like I'm that type of person. To slow down and commit and forgo everything else I've got laid out in front of me."

"What else is out there Will?"

"That's the point. I don't know. And givin Marlene everything she wants feels ass backwards when I don't even know what I want at this point."

"Have you ever talked to Mar about this?"

"No."

"Why not?"

"I dunno. How do you put words to something like this? I'm doing a poor ass job as it is here with you."

"Have you ever thought that maybe letting go a little bit and opening up to Mar and telling her all this might be good for you? Might be good for you and her both?"

"I can't put all this shit on her. She don't deserve that."

"Do you?"

Will made eye contact with Kate and smiled a bit. They both sat on the farmhouse porch in silence as the sun carved a bright path across the sky and the cattle hollered from the pastures beyond their line of sight. Barn swallows dived down under the roof of the pullshed across the way and spread their wings once reaching bottom as they rose to their homes which were nestled into the wooden beams that hung high across the shed's ceiling. To the west a large group of starlings was forming and they started to flock as they flowed and murmured across the sky -- remaining in close proximity without colliding as if joined at the hip in some intrepid dance prepared and executed every day in the fading dusk. One minute they look like smoke and another they take upon the vestige of some pulsating organism. 

"Dirty birds those lot," said Will.

"Starlings?" said Kate.

"Yeah. Dirty birds. Eat all our feed and shit everywhere, but make for a fun evening watching 'em rise up and flock as one."

They both kept their eyes trained to the sky. Not wanting to miss a thing. 

Just then Teddy stepped out from inside the house and onto the porch. "Whatcha lookin at?"

"Shhhh," said Kate.

Teddy looked to the west and saw what Kate and Will were looking at, the pulsing flock of starlings in the settling dusk. He pulled up a porch chair and sat down between them, a bit closer to Kate than Will, and they all sat for a while like that. Nothing else to be said.

Eventually the starlings drifted out of sight and Will turned to the others. "You guys ever think bout Montana. Wyoming. Alaska?"

"How do ya mean?" said Teddy. 

"Yeah, I'm not following," said Kate.

"All that country out there. All that elevation. Mountains and streams and what's left of the frontier. Seems like those are the only places left that still got it."

"There ain't nothin out there but a big loneliness awaiting every man who thinks they need to 'head west'," said Kate. "All you boys thinkin there is somethin better out there that ain't here."

Teddy turned to look at Kate as she said this. "Where did that come from?" he said.

"I just dunno why boys can't live more in the moment," Kate said.

"Didn't we just do that? Sitting here watching them birds in the sky? I was enjoying that moment right fine if you ask me..." said Teddy.

Will took all this in and turned his eyes back towards the flock of starlings which had grown small in the westward skies. 

"That was for five minutes cowboy," said Kate. "Ever tried trying to spend each day in a mindset like that? Be a lot happier if you just slowed down and stopped thinkin bout tomorrow and all that," she was flustered while saying this, shying away from Teddy and Will in her porch chair.

"Come on now Kate. We're just talkin. I hear ya, I promise I do," said Teddy while putting his hand on Kate's leg reassuringly. 

"You make a good point Kate. And I appreciate havin you around to pull Teddy and I out of these daydreams. Pull us back to reality and what we got right here in front of us," said Will with a smile. "You guys wanna step out to the pasture and see the cattle a bit 'fore it gets too dark?"

"Finally, a good idea," Kate said while rising to her feet. 

They all rose and stepped off the porch, making their way across the lawn and towards the pasture gate. Will undid the chain that wound around the post holding the gate closed and let Teddy through. Kate followed shortly behind and she made eye contact with Will as she stepped through. Almost seeming to forget something, she stopped and turned to him, throwing her arms around his shoulders into a big hug. 

"Oh...Kate...what's wrong?" said Will.

"Nothin. There ain't nothin wrong Will," she said with sparkling eyes rimmed with happy tears. She let go and stepped back and ran to Ted, taking his hand and pulling him out through the tall pasture grass and towards the herd of cattle in the near distance.
